LanguageData.XST_Lirosa.label: Lirosa
LanguageData.XST_Lirosa.description: (Lirosa's attitude towards the colony is between original Cassandra and Phoebe, she prefers peace development and trading between factions.) Jaeger There was once a space pirate group whose targets were not villages, but other pirate forces. They devoured these groups and reorganized them, slowly becoming a military force. After massive expansion, they became an unstoppable force, constantly pillaging. Due to their quick and safe method of earning money, they attracted a large number of people. Eventually, they could only rob those who could no longer sustain their huge organization. They began to reform and evolve, eventually becoming the Jaeger we know today. The social structure of the Jaeger is very simple and only divided into three classes: soldiers, workers, and slaves. The soldiers include all combat units and officers, while the workers consist of all production and trade-related personnel. The slaves are the people and their descendants from conquered areas who are treated as property by the Jaeger. Their social status, apart from that of slaves, is basically the same and their income is distributed by the military government. The Jaeger have abandoned the system of leaders and are now governed by a military government composed of seven generals. Except for financial and major decisions, they have the autonomy to make individual decisions in their respective domains and territories. There is no term limit, and if a general resigns or passes away, their superior will take over their position. The Jaeger also have an independent military court to regulate the behavior of soldiers. The Jaeger have a wide range of economic sources which includes arms trading, drug trafficking, and slave trading. Among them, the services of mercenaries and special mission contracts are particularly popular because they do not concern themselves with reasons or morals, nor do they consider politics or diplomacy. They only consider the danger of the mission and the amount of money involved. As long as there is detailed mission information and sufficient funds, the employer will have a powerful army at their disposal. The Jaeger also resort to any means necessary to seize markets and production lines, however, this behavior has sparked numerous rebellions, with Diamond Bone being their biggest enemy. Although the Jaeger have been highly regulated, their essence is still that of pirates. Commanders would allow soldiers to burn, kill and loot after capturing a stronghold, and even the military court tolerates these actions. Therefore, in the eyes of other forces, the Jaeger are nothing more than a mutated pirate gang. This has led to their foreign relations being consistently unfavorable. In such circumstances, young officers believe that the operating methods of the Jaeger need to change. They have organized a group called the "Peace Messenger", hoping to gradually change the outside world's perception of them. Lirosa Lirosa was born into a military family of the Jaeger. Her parents were a handsome man and a beautiful woman. Her father was a war hero known as the "Eagle Eye," while her mother was a torturer. Her father originally belonged to the resistance army, but joined the Jaeger after meeting her mother. Lirosa also has a twin sister named Evan, who looks very different from their family and is considered ugly. Their father told her that it was due to genetic defects that made Evan look like that and warned her not to harm her sister. Lirosa never had such thoughts because even though Evan looked ugly, she was very intelligent. She could understand how things worked just by looking at them and was like a genius, making her an idol for Lirosa. Since their birth, their mother resigned from her position as a torturer and took care of them most of the time because their father was often away due to military duties. Their mother had originally planned for Lirosa to become a welfare officer when she grew up since she was kind-hearted and empathetic even towards insects that she couldn't bear to kill. However, one day, when Lirosa was 5 years old, her mother found out that she had stolen her father's sniper rifle to shoot at targets and was hitting every shot with precision. It was then that her mother realized that she was not an ordinary child. Due to being from a military family and being beautiful and talented, Lirosa was forced to become a key training project, while Evan, despite being intelligent, was ignored due to her lack of physical abilities. Everything that Lirosa possessed made Evan very envious, and Lirosa, feeling guilty about this, promised to make her the chief of staff when she grew up. Evan was delighted to hear this and worked hard to fulfill this promise. Seeing Evan's intelligence and aptitude in intellectual tasks, their mother decided to send her to study at the Star Explorer's Academy when she was older. When Lirosa was 7 years old, their mother decided to take Evan with her to the Star Explorer's Academy for enrollment procedures. Before they left, she hugged Evan goodbye, but little did they know that it would be their final farewell. Their mother and Evan's travel party were attacked by the Diamond Bone forces on the way, and all of the soldiers escorting them were killed. Both of them went missing from that day onwards. During that time, Lirosa saw her father continuously blaming himself for what happened, and she tried to comfort him, but the situation only got worse. Eventually, their father resigned from his position as an instructor and locked himself in his room all day. Lirosa became very worried, but she still believed that there would be a miracle because in her heart, Evan and their mother were still alive. After a few years, a FedEx courier arrived at their home with a handmade and exquisite sofa. Lirosa didn't understand why it was delivered to them, so she placed it in the living room. Unexpectedly, their father burst into tears when he saw the sofa, muttering to himself incessantly. Lirosa knew something was wrong and sought help from a doctor and their father's friends. After receiving psychological counseling and comfort, their father became calmer, started to recover, and began talking to Lirosa again. However, this change made Lirosa even more uneasy because she knew that there must be some problem with the sofa. Nevertheless, she didn't dare to ask about it to prevent her father from getting upset again. Finally, one day, Lirosa saw her father measuring the sofa using a ruler. She finally asked him why he was doing that, but her father ignored her. A few days later, her father gave her a winged emblem and told her to find her sister. He then sat on the sofa and committed suicide with a gun. After losing her parents, Lirosa was assigned to live and study at a military officer training facility by the government. Many officers showed her special care due to her father's reputation, and her shooting talent was demonstrated during her time at the training facility. At only 14 years old, she went from being a trainee to becoming a sniper training officer, with some officers even praising her skills as surpassing those of her father. Lirosa served as a training officer for less than a year before voluntarily requesting to be deployed to the front lines in response to the resistance army's attack on the Jaegers. This decision was made by Lirosa herself, and not at the military's request. She knew that staying in the restrictive training facility would keep the winged emblem her father gave her forever a mystery. The reality of the battlefield was vastly different from what Lirosa had imagined. She used to think that getting shot meant just falling down and bleeding, but on the front lines, the scenes were filled with carnage and gore from heavy artillery fire. After experiencing the baptism of war, she became serious and silent. She knew that any mistake could mean failing to fulfill her father's dying wish. After participating in over a dozen battles as a sniper, Lirosa achieved a terrifying record. Her fearsome reputation did not come from how many people she killed, but rather from her zero-kill record. When faced with the enemy, Lirosa found that she couldn't bring herself to take a life. Each time before pulling the trigger, she would think about her mother and Evan. With one movement of her finger, the enemy's family would suffer the same fate. She decided to effectively stop their actions without killing them, becoming known as the terrifying Knee-Destroyer. Her god-like spatial awareness, predictive ability, dynamic vision, and four-dimensional bladder, combined with her father's double-tap sniper technique, made her capable of bringing down enemies in any position, whether walking, running, crawling, or even riding heavy machinery. She even caused some enemies to wear helmets on their knees from fear of her. Due to this incident, Lirosa quickly became a sensation and was subsequently used for propaganda by the military. She was referred to as the "Queen" who made enemies kneel, as her cold and stern demeanor perfectly suited the title. This nickname spread quickly, causing panic among the resistance army whenever they learned that the Queen had arrived. Some even fell to their knees in fear of being shot in the knee. However, these immobilized casualties and soldiers posed a significant resource drain and psychological burden on the resistance army. The frequent complaints of those shot in the knee - "I can only be a guard in the future" - filled the entire army with fear. As time passed, most of them lost their will to fight and surrendered, and the war turned into sporadic conflicts. At the end of the war, the military's official media heavily promoted Lirosa as the one who quelled the war in two years, and at the age of 17, she was honored as a war hero and referred to as the "Queen" by others. From then on, Lirosa enjoyed the treatment of a hero, receiving a fixed monthly bonus and having the freedom to choose to participate in various missions and military affairs. Afterwards, Lirosa chose to join the "Peace Messenger", an organization established to change the public's perception of Jaegers. It was primarily responsible for public relations work and also conducted charitable activities. The organization had flexible working hours, which suited her nature better, and more importantly, she could gather a lot of information while working. At this point, Lirosa could finally focus on investigating the Winged Emblem. Unfortunately, things were not so simple. Although she spent most of her bonus on intelligence gathering and even came into contact with XEO assassins, no one knew the source of this mysterious emblem. The only thing left uninvestigated was the hostile Diamond Bone force. Due to the military government's sensitivity to Diamond Bone-related matters, mishandling it could lead to being treated as a spy. Originally, Lirosa hoped to find other entry points, but it seemed impossible. After careful consideration, she unilaterally leaked information about the emblem to Diamond Bone and secretly observed them. A few weeks later, a lone member of the resistance army appeared on the Jaeger's border. His sniping was so precise that he was able to shoot two consecutive shots, hitting his targets in places that were too gruesome to look at. Men were shot in their testicles or had their heads blown off, while women had their breasts and genitals shot, rendering them sterile even if they managed to survive. This terrifying style of shooting immediately reminded people of the late sharpsniper Eagle Eye, who happened to be Lirosa's father. However, since Eagle Eye had already passed away, the military believed that someone was imitating him to affect the morale of the troops. As time went on, the situation became increasingly serious. The enemy's strength was almost on par with that of Eagle Eye, causing a large number of casualties among the soldiers. Despite the Jaeger's daily bombing, it had no effect, leaving the military with no choice but to order the female soldiers to retreat from the border first. Although the military made efforts to suppress the spread of the incident, Lirosa quickly obtained intelligence from her role as a Peace Messenger. She believed that the sniper must be connected to her father and offered to fight for the cause, but the military refused to let her participate, fearing it would damage their image if the "Queen" were insulted. In the end, Lirosa had to exercise her hero's right to demand permission from the local general to fight. Moved by her determination, the general agreed, but the risks of sniper ambushes were high due to the unpredictability of the battlefield. If anything unexpected happened, the general could not explain it to the other six generals. Therefore, he proposed a condition to Lirosa: if the opponent agreed to a duel, she could fight. Soon after, the border troops broadcasted the rules of the duel. If the opponent fired two shots in succession, it would mean they agreed to the duel. As a result, the soldier holding the loudspeaker and the officer leading the troops both became eunuchs. The soldiers who knew about the upcoming battle between the "Queen" and "Eagle Eye" were filled with excitement, causing the local popcorn and soda stocks to sell out instantly. Before the duel, both parties presented their demands and received agreement from each other. The mysterious sniper requested that if he won, the Jaeger would return the territory and promise never to attack again. The Jaeger, on the other hand, demanded complete dissolution of the resistance army and submission to the military government. The rules of the duel were very simple: only the Red Line rifle provided by the committee could be used, and the contestants could only wear leather or cloth armor. Both parties had to stand at attention holding their rifles and be ready at a distance of 100 meters apart on the plain. They could only begin shooting after the signal was given. The duel would continue until one side died or lost the ability to fight, and the winner would have the right to execute the defeated opponent. The stakes were high for Lirosa, as the mysterious sniper had already threatened to kill the "Queen" and disrupt morale. However, having lost everything, she didn't care about the risks. She only focused on her father's last words that her sister might still be alive, and this opponent was the key. Therefore, winning was imperative for her. Finally, the day of the duel had arrived. The vast plain was packed with onlookers, and there were more members of the resistance army who had surrendered than there were Jaeger soldiers present. During the war, many members of the resistance army understood that they could easily be killed by a mere shot from Queen Lirosa's gun, and that she could take them out one by one. However, instead of killing them, she aimed for their knees which saved them during the brutal battles. Despite their fear of the Queen, the resistance army deeply appreciated this female hero. Many of those who had been shot in the knees even volunteered to maintain order and guard the area, all for the chance to catch a glimpse of the legendary Queen. Due to concerns for the safety of their heroes, the military government strictly prohibited the release of any related images or photos. In sniper situations, Lirosa was essentially a legend, and some even speculated that the knee damage was not caused by a single sniper. Such speculation only increased people's curiosity about her. The military government also recognized the resistance army's view towards Lirosa, which is why they allowed her to appear in public with her true identity. They even built temporary spectator stands for the unprecedented number of viewers present in order to change their perception of the government. On that day, Lirosa appeared in a tight-fitting grey and black leather suit, causing a commotion throughout the audience. No one had expected the queen who joined the military to be such an exquisite beauty, and the military realized that allowing her to appear was worth it, as long as they could win this duel to achieve their goal. The mysterious sniper who followed Lirosa onto the field was much more plain, wearing a brown leather coat with a hooded headpiece to prevent future assassination attempts. As the two inspected their weapons on the open plain, the audience could see that Lirosa's outfit was highly disadvantageous for her, while the bulky leather coat of the mysterious sniper could serve to disrupt vision, causing them to secretly worry about Lirosa. The two faced each other in the center of the field and shook hands. The mysterious sniper told Lirosa that he couldn't care less about her looks and would blow her away, but the cold-hearted Lirosa ignored him and walked back to her starting position. After they both prepared themselves, the whole arena fell silent. Lirosa's father had explained to her the key points of this shooting technique, which focused on consciousness and muscle movement during shooting. Therefore, as long as one predicts correctly when seeing the opponent, they can use micro movements to evade. The Eagle Eye imposter was also very aware of this, so he wore a leather jacket, which made it difficult for his hands and knees to be accurately observed. He even chose the body axis, which cannot make micro movements, as his shooting target. As the signal gun went off, both sides raised their guns and fired a shot blindly. At that moment, the audience screamed as the Queen's breast was pierced from the side, causing a bunch of mosaic to burst out. Although Lirosa had predicted the hit location and dodged sideways, she could not escape being hit. The force of the bullet pulled her curvaceous body down to the ground. On the other hand, the mysterious sniper remained unharmed, only the weapon he held in his hand, which he could not avoid when aiming and attacking, was hit. Looking at the gun in his hand that had become trash, the mysterious sniper secretly admired Lirosa's intelligence and courage. However, one had no idea whether she was dead or alive, while the other had a weapon that had been shattered. As the military forces of the conference, they did not know how to judge the outcome for a while. The whole venue erupted in intense discussion, and the audience was worried about the Queen who had been pierced in the breast because the hollow effect of the Red Line rifle was notoriously powerful. From the mosaic on the ground, they knew the situation was very serious and there was a risk to her life at any time. However, the mysterious sniper, who had lost his weapon, was now cursing her shamelessness. The audience immediately felt ashamed of him and those concerned about the Queen's injury began to argue with him. The mysterious sniper kept arguing, but all he got in return were more curses. Even the resistance army started booing at him. Finally, unable to withstand the pressure, he turned off the mosaic effect. When the mosaic disappeared, the audience saw a bunch of bursting sponges. It turned out that Lirosa had padded her B-cup breasts into D-cups with padding. This is a trick she learned from some female soldiers who survived mysterious sniper attacks, the tight-fitting suit she wears is not just for looks, but in direct contrast to the concealment provided by the mysterious sniper's leather jacket, it actually causes opponents to make mistakes in their shooting calculations. After learning the truth, the angry and pleasantly surprised audience started to clamor for a rematch and showered the venue with popcorn and soft drinks. The military had a hard time calming down the audience's emotions, but eventually managed to do so and announced that the queen had won. Lirosa had just recovered from a dangerous duel, spared the mysterious sniper and handed him over to the military for interrogation. The Queen also gave a gift to the resistance army, announcing that if they were willing to pay taxes to the government, the land would be returned to them and slavery abolished. They would also receive protection from the Jaeger. This was the victory agreement proposed by her to the government and something the "Peace Messenger" had always wanted to do. Upon hearing this, the resistance army was moved to tears and no longer had to kneel down due to getting shot in the knee. After this incident, Lirosa's charisma made the military realize that cooperation, rather than war and power, may be a better choice. From then on, the Queen's reputation spread throughout the borderlands, and some villages even voluntarily joined the Jaeger. The government also changed its domestic rules to accommodate this development. Although many frontline soldiers still held onto their bandit-like thinking, and the problems left by the war could not be solved overnight, these things began to slowly change. After Lirosa returned to the Peace Messenger, she was unable to contact the mysterious sniper due to the military's obstruction. Although she was very anxious about this, her duel had become a legend among the Jaeger, and she was quickly becoming known as the "Queen." Therefore, she did not want to further stimulate the military by taking action. Several weeks later, Lirosa received a notification from the government requesting that she take over the interrogation of the mysterious sniper. This sudden change left her filled with doubts until she saw her father alive in the interrogation room. Shocked by this revelation, Lirosa immediately proposed conditions to the military - no recording and isolation - which they surprisingly agreed to on the spot. They only asked her to submit a detailed report afterwards. They were then arranged in a secret room, and the military handed Lirosa a bag before leaving and closing the door. Without saying a word, Lirosa took out her winged emblem. The mysterious sniper widened his eyes upon seeing it and took out an opposite winged emblem from the bag, forming a pair of wings. Lirosa realized that the knot in her heart was about to be untied. During their conversation, Lirosa learned that the person in front of her was her uncle. He informed her that the family's genes had been altered, and so they were destined to have twins. He was the real "Eagle Eye," while Lirosa's father had only intentionally joined the Jaeger as a spy using his name. Eagle Eye told Lirosa that her parents truly loved each other, and after her father expressed his intentions, he stopped sending information. It was always him working alone to cover up the intelligence work. Although Eagle Eye had retired, he came back into the picture upon receiving news of the winged emblem and realizing that his brother had met with misfortune. He returned to prevent himself from being suspected. When Lirosa asked about the sofa, the situation took a sudden turn for the worse. Her uncle seemed like a different person, his voice becoming low and solemn. He countered by asking if she found the sofa comfortable to sit on, and thereafter refused to answer any more questions about it. Finally, he admitted that he was uncertain about the whereabouts of Lirosa's sister and refused to disclose his organization, only insisting that Jaeger execute him publicly. At this point, Lirosa sensed that Eagle Eye was already consumed by a deep fear. Later, Lirosa reported everything truthfully to the military except for the genetic modifications. Although there were no major breakthroughs, the military was very satisfied, and in accordance with Eagle Eye's wishes, they executed him publicly. In the following months, Lirosa conducted investigations into genetic modification and the phenomenon of Sofa. All the clues pointed to Diamond Bone, a mysterious and secretive faction. It was then that she finally discovered why her father and Eagle Eye were so terrified. Rumors had it that Diamond Bone turned the family members of traitors into Sofa as a warning. If the traitor did not die, they would continue to turn their loved ones into Sofa until there were no more left. Lirosa's father had committed suicide to protect her from this fate. Upon realizing this, she returned home and wept in her mother(Sofa), vowing to find her sister. One day, a few months later, Lirosa received information that a doctor was offering all of his assets to hire mercenaries. Intrigued, she hurried to the scene and saw that the doctor was not offering a small sum of money. However, he was still desperately seeking a six-person team. Lirosa was full of questions and asked the military personnel on duty to find out that he wanted to attack a Diamond Bone laboratory. Without any mission intelligence, no one was willing to join. Puzzled, Lirosa personally asked the doctor for more information and explained the difficulties, but he remained tight-lipped as if hiding something. Lirosa's intuition told her that there might be news about her sister in the laboratory, so she volunteered. Many soldiers flocked to join after seeing the Queen's involvement, and they eventually formed a 12-member squad. They formed a travel team and arrived at their destination a few days later. However, after reconnaissance, they realized that the situation was perilous. All of the laboratory guards were warriors wearing platinum power armor, and they also found XEO assassins, with a minimum of 90 people. Lirosa decided to cancel the mission and promised to return the doctor's hiring fee, compensating the team with her own reward. The doctor cried, saying that his daughter was doomed. Lirosa sympathized but understood the insurmountable difference in military strength, so she could only console the weeping father. As they were preparing to return, they saw a white smoke rising on the horizon, accompanied by a series of shouts - "For the Queen" - which made Lirosa feel embarrassed and ashamed. Soon, a massive army appeared before her eyes. Apparently, her mission had been leaked, attracting a large number of troops to this location. In the end, the Queen led a force of nearly 300 people to launch an attack. The battle did not last long, as some Diamond Bone soldiers began to panic and flee due to the overwhelming number of enemies, with only a few fanatical assassins still fighting. Diamond Bone soldiers began to burn down the laboratory and kill the researchers and test subjects. Seeing this, the Doctor became frantic and rushed into the lab. Lirosa could not stop him and could only follow in with over ten people. Screaming could be heard coming from the lab. Lirosa ordered them to prioritize finding the Doctor, but soon discovered that he had already died. Lirosa felt very sad and could only do her best to help the remaining people. However, the scene was chaotic, and the Diamond Bone soldiers did not even notice them, just slaughtering the test subjects and committing suicide. Lirosa's knee strikes were also ineffective, as they knelt down and committed suicide on the spot. The temperature at the scene also began to rise, so Lirosa ordered a retreat to protect the safety of her team members. After they left, the laboratory burned for several tens of minutes before collapsing. Lirosa's unit only managed to save a few test subjects in the end, all of them being human-shaped pillows with their limbs removed. Lirosa approached them with a hopeful expression, inquiring about her sister's whereabouts, only to find that their vocal cords and teeth had been removed. They didn't even listen to Lirosa's words but continuously begged for death through their eyes and expressions. Lirosa knew full well the practices of Jaeger, and that the military would not spend resources on providing prosthetics for these types of captives. Instead, they would be treated as entertainment items and sold, ultimately destined to be tortured to death by their buyers. Despite knowing the consequences of violating military regulations, Lirosa still forcibly rescued them because she wanted to know about her sister's whereabouts. At this moment, she realized her mistake. Although Lirosa understood the consequences of her actions, her error was driven by personal motives, and only she could put an end to this tragedy. On this day, the Queen finally broke the record of zero kills. The soldiers were shocked to see the queen's actions, as the consequences of disposing of captives privately are severe, and everyone was discussing it fervently. The news of the queen breaking the record of zero kills quickly spread among the Jaeger. To everyone's surprise, this well-intentioned violation led soldiers from various regions to sign a petition to plead with their respective generals on behalf of the queen, leaving the generals shocked. Despite this, Lirosa knew she would receive heavy punishment from the military court. No one was saved during the mission, the employer had died, and she acquired no information about her sister. It left her feeling utterly defeated. Soon after, Lirosa received an invitation from the Seven Generals, the highest level of meeting within Jaeger. She knew it must have been related to the mission. That day, she entered the meeting room nervously, where only the Seven Generals were present, and no other officers. The atmosphere was so tense that it was suffocating. When the generals saw her, they stood up without saying a word and slowly walked toward her, kneeling down in front of her. Lirosa was startled, and stepped back until she exited the conference hall and slowly closed the door. One of the generals nervously kicked the door open and carried her back to the central seat in the hall. The other generals, sweating profusely from the scare, returned to their respective seats. As Lirosa gradually regained her composure, the eldest general began to slowly tell the story of the Jaeger. Originally, the Jaeger was just a group of eight righteous hunters whose goal was to dismantle the space pirates and absorb and teach them to do good. To effectively manage the organization, they elected a leader. However, as the organization grew larger, they felt pressure due to the lack of management expertise. In addition, the recruits were mainly uneducated robbers with no culture, and their only source of income was through robbery. At this point, some members suggested dissolving the organization, but the leader, who had already succumbed to his own power, disagreed and resorted to piracy in order to maintain the organization. His behavior awakened the nature of the members and the organization soon turned from justice to evil. After many years, many forces were forced to submit to the massive power of the organization. The leader had lost control and proclaimed himself the "Pirate King". The other seven members conspired to kill him and then jointly managed the organization, proclaiming the abolition of the leader system. They thought they could improve the situation, but they were wrong. The leader's ideology had deeply rooted in them and could not be changed back. Dissolving the organization would be like releasing wild animals, so they had to establish rules and manage it. However, the fundamental problem still existed. During their discussions, someone suggested creating another "Pirate King", but they realized that what they needed was not a leader who led others, but a hero who made others reflect on themselves. From then on, these seven people became hero makers. They searched for suitable candidates and secretly molded them into heroes to serve as spiritual leaders for the robbers and guide them back to righteousness. However, they found that this was not easy because they needed more than just suitable candidates. They needed to create heroes at appropriate timing. Therefore, they were only able to create heroes, but not super-heroes during their time. Thus, the seven decided to pass on their responsibilities to their successors and let them carry on the tradition. Therefore, the generals had long known that Lirosa's father was a fake "Eagle Eye" and that her mother, who served as a torturer for "Eagle Eye," was also arranged by them. The spy mission actually helped them because they needed heroes - creating heroes was what they were supposed to do. That is why Lirosa's past actions and requests went smoothly. Originally, the generals strictly prohibited informing those involved in hero-making to prevent abuse of power and another "Pirate King" from emerging. Lirosa was able to learn about the top secret information because of her personality and influence. From the violation of military regulations in this incident, the generals saw that Lirosa could very likely become the superhero who would bring the entire Jaeger back on track. Therefore, they decided to inform her of the confidential information and made it clear that she was the highest decision-maker. They warned her not to engage in any activity that could endanger her life, and the punishment from the court was only symbolic - prohibiting her from participating in large-scale missions. Now, Lirosa knows that she is no longer living just for herself but also for the future of the entire Jaeger. This responsibility will eventually become an invisible shackle for her, and as someone who has lost her freedom, she may never find her sister again. *Lirosa, who is naturally kind and serious, no longer participates in dangerous missions because she bears a huge responsibility. Therefore, her story is relatively mild and does not involve too much fighting.
LanguageData.XST_Vic.label: Vic
LanguageData.XST_Vic.description: (Vic's attitude towards the colony is like original Cassandra，and he prefers events happening in the world.) Star Explorer A highly influential faction, originally just a group of scientists who were persecuted by religion and politics. However, several of them possessed expertise in aerospace technology and extraterrestrial mining, and they led other scientists while also incorporating exiles who had been banished from elsewhere. They quickly developed into a high-tech organization and attracted numerous investors, ultimately forming what is now known as the Star Explorer. The Star Explorer have evolved into a democratic country, with the Star Explorer Council serving as their core governing body. This council is composed of 48 members and one chairman, with each member serving a term of five years. All members are appointed and elected by citizens, and any decision must receive support from 27 or more members to pass. However, due to factors such as factional struggles and financial flows, the operation of the system appears fair on the surface but is actually quite dark. The Star Explorers believe that knowledge is power, which is why they invest heavily in technological development and extraterrestrial exploration. They have also established the "Star Explorer Database" to collect and study technology. Additionally, they collaborate with other factions, including Diamond Bone. The Star Explorer fully utilizes the economic value of their military assets and soldiers. They create chaos and incite conflicts among other factions, and then export weapons or send troops to target factions to increase their influence. This has always been the development strategy of the Star Explorer. However, this behavior has drawn the attention of XEO, who retaliated by targeting them and assassinating eight council members within a week. This incident directly led to the employment of Jaeger by the Star Explorer to suppress XEO in the long term. To maintain social stability, the Star Explorer exiles serious criminals and their families to other regions. If military personnel or special police discover exiles who have returned within the Star Explorer's territory, they are authorized to execute them on the spot without reporting to their superiors. Some of the exiles, due to their hatred towards the Star Explorer, have formed the Flame Legion and launched attacks against the Star Explorer's strongholds, causing a large number of civilian casualties. The conflict between the Star Explorer and the Flame Legion has never ceased, and the population of exiles continues to increase. Some commentators believe that some politicians intentionally create threats to the Star Explorer to gain greater political leverage. Vic Vic's parents were both archaeologists, and his mother gave birth to him while on an archaeological expedition. Without even a birth certificate, Vic was raised by his parents who frequently moved for work and didn't have much time to take care of him. As compensation, they bought him lots of indie games to play with. Through these games, Vic learned many survival skills, but what interested him the most was "mining." He often sneaked into excavation sites when his parents weren't looking and dug away. Vic had good luck and often found special items, but he knew they were artifacts that could affect his parents' work, so he never took them. One day, Vic dug up a black stone in the ruins of an ancient city. As he gazed at the stone, it seemed like his soul was sucked into it, which made him feel amazing. Breaking his own rule, he decided to take it with him. For a long period after that, the archaeology team remained at the same site, seemingly searching for something. There were often some cool-looking people coming and going, whom Vic's parents told him were assassins, warning him not to provoke them. Vic, who had been waiting for too long, became increasingly bored and secretly took out the black stone to observe it. He hadn't shown the stone to his parents yet because he was afraid of being scolded. As he gazed at the stone, Vic could feel that it had some kind of consciousness. He tried talking to the stone in his mind, and although it didn't respond, he felt like he understood what the stone was thinking. It was as if he were talking to himself. From then on, whenever Vic felt bored, he would talk to the stone. Because he followed his parents around and couldn't make friends, the stone became his only friend. A few months later, the archaeology team arrived at a new excavation site. Vic's parents stayed in the campsite most of the time to plan their archaeological schedule and route. One day, Vic absentmindedly took out the black stone, but his parents were terrified when they noticed it. His father panicked and pulled out a metal box from his backpack, telling Vic to stand still and not move. Vic stood frozen in place, watching as his father closed his eyes, grabbed the stone, and placed it in the box. Vic thought he would be scolded for taking out the stone, but to his surprise, his parents gave him a thumbs up and promised to settle down on the Star Explorer, no longer living the life of wandering archaeologists. Excited, Vic shouted in joy. At that moment, Vic realized that even though he had lost the stone, that "friend" seemed to still linger in his mind. Not long after, Vic's parents became anxious and suggested the archaeological team pack up their gear and return to the Star Explorer as soon as possible. They spent the entire night organizing their travel group and set off the next morning. Unfortunately, disaster struck soon after they departed when they were attacked by the Flame Legion's troops. The guards were all killed, and the rest were either killed or captured. Vic was frightened and prayed that someone would come to rescue them. That night, a group of people came to their aid, but the situation quickly turned chaotic. Taking advantage of the chaos, Vic grabbed some food and fled. He became separated from his parents and returned to the territory of the Star Explorer alone. At just five years old and without his parents, Vic was forced to survive on the streets and play games like playing PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds (PUBG). After two years of living this harsh life, he was eventually taken into an orphanage by a welfare officer of the Star Explorer. Through the baptism of fire, Vic became filled with killing intent. The other orphans in the orphanage thought he was crazy and avoided him, and he never paid attention to anyone else either. During his days at the orphanage, Vic saw a staggeringly ugly girl being bullied every day by a group of people, including the guards who turned a blind eye. Although Vic had thought about intervening, the bullies were all tall young men and there were too many of them. His two years of playing PUBG taught him not to get involved in such situations. However, one day, the group began physically assaulting the girl after verbally abusing her, and eventually, she lost an eye and was close to death. The guards did nothing but hold melons instead of sticks. Unable to bear it any longer, Vic finally stepped in front of the girl and ordered the big guys to stop. The young men were initially hesitant, but they followed suit and started attacking him as well. Vic had no choice but to fight back against ten people. However, reality proved to be cruel, and he ended up in the hospital for three months after being hit on the head with a glass bottle and a watermelon. He couldn't remember anything after that and was questioned by the police. The doctors believed it was likely a concussion and told him not to worry. After Vic recovered, he wasn't sent back to the orphanage. The reason was due to the fight at the orphanage. The judge sentenced him to attend a military school for training to give back to society, as he had caused serious injury and accidental death. Years later, Vic graduated from the military school with excellent grades and joined the special police force as an Executor. With his agile thinking and rich experience playing PUBG, he quickly became a superstar in the law enforcement community. He solved several difficult cases and stopped multiple attempts by XEO to assassinate others. His skillful use of dual guns also became his trademark, earning him the nickname "Evil Gunner". However, during this time, Vic's amnesia still occurred occasionally. Whenever he became emotional, his memory would become blank. He even completed one operation without knowing how. This made Vic increasingly afraid, but seeking medical help didn't provide any answers. The psychologists only told him that it was because he hadn't taken his medication that day. Fortunately, his condition wasn't severe and his colleagues didn't notice anything wrong. Vic had no family and was plagued by amnesia, felt a sense of loss every day. He often thought about his parents, and the feeling grew stronger over time. After considering it for a few days, Vic believed that he had the ability to find his parents, so he decided to investigate what had happened in the past and set a goal for himself. He quickly found reports on the attack against the archaeological team, which showed that no one survived the attack by the Flame Legion. The report didn't mention any rescue efforts, which conflicted with his memories. This made him question the appearance of a team on that day. However, as many years had passed, he couldn't find any leads to follow up on and could only explore the situation with an open mind. Soon after, the intelligence division of the special police force received a message containing a meaningless article signed by a staggeringly ugly little girl. The intelligence division believed it was a prank and disposed of the article as garbage. However, Vic was intrigued by the signature and, with his experience in games, quickly discovered that the article had spacing issues between paragraphs. He secretly made a copy of the information, and after the article was rearranged, the spaces between the lines formed a set of numbers. He immediately realized that it was a news code and searched for it. Vic found a news report on the attack against a Jaeger's travel team, which was similar to the incident involving the archaeological team, but the perpetrators were from the Diamond Bone army instead. The signature also reminded him of the girl from the orphanage. Using such a complicated method may have been an attempt to remain discreet. Vic then investigated the source of the message and found that it came from the "Peace Messenger", a public relations agency for the Jaeger. Perhaps the girl's family was trying to find her through this agency. With his helpful attitude, Vic decided to take on the case. He went back to the orphanage, but upon arrival, he found that it had been turned into a fried chicken shop. After searching around, he discovered that all relevant people and records had disappeared. The officials and staff of the orphanage were either dead or missing. Vic knew that this was not a simple matter, but without any clues, he didn't know where to start. In the end, Vic got inspired by the signature of the article. The unique appearance of the staggeringly ugly little girl would surely have left a deep impression on those who had seen her. Perhaps he could find a clue from there. With this idea in mind, Vic focused his attention on the village where the orphanage was located. When he first approached the village chief, the chief refused to speak about the orphanage. It wasn't until Vic threatened him with Heroic Behavior(search everything in village) that the chief reluctantly answered. However, the chief didn't know anything about the orphanage. He did remember the girl with the strange appearance, though, because someone famous had brought her to the village at the time. That person was a serial killer known as "Scream". This serial killer caused quite a stir among the Star Explorer community. He tortured and killed his victims, then used their remains to create works of art. Being staggeringly ugly from birth himself, he sought out attractive individuals as his targets. It's possible that the girl's appearance may have sparked his empathy, which saved her life. Once Vic knew who the killer was, being in a position of authority made things easier. After searching through records, he discovered that the killer had been executed several years prior. During his lifetime, he had close contact with a member of a special operations team, which had since been disbanded due to all members being killed in action. Though there were no detailed records available, Vic knew that this team was involved with the Twin Star project, a program designed to train spies. With this information, Vic began to suspect that the archaeological and travel teams' attacks were not rescue missions, but rather calculated murders committed by members of this special operations team. In fact, Vic had long known about the Star Explorer's intentional creation of conflicts between various factions. Although this was a shameful act, he could not let the Jaegers know the truth about the attack on their travel team. Given the fanatical nature of the Jaegers, mishandling the situation could easily lead to war. As for the incident involving the archaeological team, the key might lie with the black stone that his parents took away. Vic felt that someone within the Star Explorers wanted to erase all information related to this stone. Only as an unidentified person with a nickname was he able to avoid being targeted. At this point, Vic felt that his parents may have been killed, but without knowing the truth, he held onto hope. In the following days, Vic focused all his energy on finding out what had happened to the girl. He saw it as a way of compensating her family for what they had been through. However, he soon discovered that the matter was far more complicated than he initially thought. The issues involved were beyond his authorized access, and he had to engage in many unauthorized activities to uncover the truth. It turned out that Diamond Bone had been purchasing orphans from the orphanage for human experimentation conducted by the Star Explorers. Vic also discovered the location where the girl had been taken for experimentation. However, when he was on his way to the facility, it was attacked by Jaegers and destroyed. The remaining captives were killed by a notorious murderer known as "The Queen." In the end, no one survived, and Vic lamented that if not for the girl, he would have met the same fate. Although he felt a sense of loss, the matter came to a close, and all he could do was pray for the girl's soul to rest in peace. After some time had passed, a major event occurred in Diamond Bone. A woman known as "The Dragon" led a rebellion, and there were people supporting her cause all over the country. This put the nation at risk of collapsing. Under the long-standing rule of fear by the "Circle," it seemed like a normal result of unrest. However, Vic felt that the process was too smooth and began to investigate the identities of those who supported the rebellion. He discovered that some of the leaders were not native to Diamond Bone but had joined for various reasons. This reminded Vic of the special operations team, which may still be active but operating under a hidden identity. Vic thought about threatening them to obtain information, but soon learned that they had all died in the war. This indicated that whoever was manipulating events was cautious and ruthless, treating their subordinates as disposable pawns. Vic connected the dots, believing that the "Scream" incident resulted in a death sentence because the girl was spared. The orphanage was chosen because of that same girl, and perhaps his parents had already been killed. Vic was repulsed by the manipulator's ruthless behavior of trying to eradicate everything, and he pledged to find the manipulator and make them pay for their actions. He secretly leaked all the information he obtained to the public and observed the reactions of all parties to locate the target. As expected, the resulting public opinion put immense pressure on the council, with members blaming each other for the situation. Just as Vic thought he could force the mastermind to reveal themselves, his plan was disrupted by a red-haired XEO girl. This woman caused panic throughout the city shortly after her debut, cutting multiple children and women in half. Later, her targets shifted to high-ranking officials and wealthy celebrities of the Star Explorer, causing all news attention to focus on her. Despite being a merciless killer, the special police in charge of security reported that she did not harm anyone outside of her target. Additionally, she was stunningly beautiful and had a long ponytail, which earned her the nickname "Red Scorpion." After the incident persisted for a while, the situation worsened. The Red Scorpion stopped issuing warnings before attacking and began conducting surprise aerial assaults. Many of the special forces knew they couldn't stop her, so they decided to watch from the sidelines instead. Some even intentionally approached wealthy clients under their protection just to catch a glimpse of her. However, these irresponsible actions were quickly corrected by the council. They temporarily took over all of the special forces' duties until the situation was resolved. During this time, Vic suffered greatly at the hands of this assassin. The information publicly available on her slowly became useless under the glare of her brilliance. As he watched the manipulator leave the spotlight and retreat back into the shadows, Vic felt helpless. He knew that he had no more chips left to pursue the matter further, so he had to give up for the time being. Months later, the council received intelligence that XEO was planning to assassinate a politician and his son. The council decided to assign the special forces to handle internal security and tasked Vic with providing close protection for the targets. This politician was someone whom Vic had known for a long time and had often provided administrative convenience for him. When the council assigned this task, Vic agreed without hesitation. The entire mission was relatively easy for Vic because there were special forces stationed at various key points in the large mansion where the targets resided. The politician had also hired a team of Jaegers and built numerous defense facilities, making it even impervious to an attack from the Flame Legion. On the day of the incident, the central room of the mansion was where Vic and the politician's son stayed, while the politician himself wore a power armor, sitting like a turtle. Vic tried to persuade him to relax, but to no avail. He then turned around and played with the politician's 5-year-old son, whom Vic loved to interact with. Vic often recounted his childhood archaeological and refugee experiences as if they were fairy tales, and the children were always captivated by his storytelling. That night, with little pressure on him, he continued to play with the child until suddenly the sound of anti-aircraft fire could be heard. Vigilant, he immediately pushed the child behind him. In a split second, a transport pod crashed through the ceiling, filling the whole room with smoke. As Vic regained his senses, a pleasant song entered his ears and a head slowly rolled towards him. The sight of the politician's head rolling on the ground in front of his son caused the child to cry out in terror. Vic immediately pulled out his dual pistols and fired wildly into the smoke, but there was no response. Seeing this, Vic reloaded his guns and prepared for battle. As the smoke cleared slightly, the elusive red hair let Vic know that the situation was not good. However, Vic was an experienced assassin and knew that the kinetic energy transfer field equipped by the assassins would overload quickly if force was applied to the same point. As the "Evil Gunner," he could fire 16 bullets into the same spot within ten meters, giving him enough confidence to take down the scorpion. Without hesitation, Vic activated the V system and fired eight bullets per second into the force field at one point, causing it to overload and shatter. Vic did not want to kill her outright, so he only shot four bullets into her arms and legs. However, the Red Scorpion exhibited superhuman speed and was able to slice through the bullets with ease, leaving behind only afterimages. This scene reminded Vic of The Matrix, and he realized that his opponent was likely using some kind of BUG or cheat techniques. As Vic was about to use his headshot hack, he noticed the weapon in Red Scorpion's hand. The airflow generated by the vibration blade could indeed deflect the bullet without destroying it, but the material of this type of blade was very unstable. Using it to chop bullets in a rough manner could potentially trigger a large explosion. Vic stopped shooting when he realized this, and the Red Scorpion also didn't move while waiting for the force field to recharge. Vic took advantage of this opportunity to examine the situation. The Red Scorpion had just demonstrated superhuman speed and could have avoided Vic's shots, so her decision to slice the bullets might have been a message of threat to him. Because an explosion in this space would surely result in death for everyone, if Vic continued to shoot, the opponent would continue to attack, and eventually both sides, including the child, would lose their lives. While pondering a solution, Vic remembered that this young lady never killed anyone except her target, and this game couldn't hit targets outside of one range in close combat. With a sudden inspiration, he took a risk and threw away his guns, placing himself between the child and the assassin. To his surprise, this strange tactic worked, and the assassin did not attack again. She just walked over to him and stared at him, creating a very awkward atmosphere between them. As they locked eyes, Vic finally realized that the rumors about her unique attractiveness were true, but he had no time to appreciate it in this situation. All he wanted was to get rid of her as soon as possible. Both sides remained deadlocked for more than ten seconds until a group of footsteps approached, causing the Red Scorpion to turn and leave. Vic finally breathed a sigh of relief as he watched her figure disappear into the distance. Although Vic saved the politician's child, he was still investigated by the council. The reason was that the location of transport pod was too accurate, and his unauthorized investigation of the orphanage incident was uncovered. Many council members accused him of being a spy, and some even slandered him as hiding behind the child out of fear. Unfortunately, the video recording of the scene was damaged and could not be presented as evidence. Besides the child, there were no other witnesses, and the child had been traumatized and unable to speak. Vic was speechless and ultimately, the council decided to strip him of his duties and exile him. Many special police officers were unhappy with the verdict, and his friends tried everything to appeal for him, but they hit a wall at every turn. Vic did not want to implicate anyone else and eventually confessed to the crime. Although Vic was sentenced to prison, he still had to fulfill his duties as a special police officer before being exiled. During this time, a friend in the police force risked his life to obtain information about an assassination plot targeting Vic. After examining the information, Vic was surprised to discover that the second target was not the councilman's son but himself. The whole assassination operation was just a political game orchestrated by the council, and it only became a media frenzy because of the Red Scorpion's failed first attempt. His friends suggested that he make the information public, but it did not reveal who the commissioner was and Vic knew how dangerous the forces behind them could be. So, he refused for everyone's safety. That night, Vic rethink the events and learned that XEO and Star Explorer had a shady deal that could help him find out the identity of the commissioner. He believed that the Red Scorpion was the key and her irrational behavior gave him the chance to contact her again. He felt that he had been pushed to this point by the manipulators and it was no longer a matter of who would pay the price, but rather who could win the chicken dinner. A strange hunger ignited within Vic, and he felt like another person was breaking through from within. In the following days, Vic was so excited that he could not sleep at night. He spent the whole night organizing clues and preparing for his journey. On the day of his exile, Vic said goodbye to each of his friends with a smile on his face. He was grateful to have such good brothers and the opportunity to leave this fancy but corrupt hole for another adventure filled with the unknown. Vic was promptly loaded onto a transport pod and ejected from the country's borders, and as soon as he landed on foreign soil, he saw people eating melons and waiting for him. These people were not kind-hearted believers, but Vic's guns were already hungry and eager. He showed an evil grin and was ready to win the chicken dinner tonight. *Vic was a person full of passion and curiosity. Due to the tragedy he experienced in his childhood and being forced to play PlayerUnknown's Battlegrounds, he developed another personality named Zeo opposite to his original character. Zeo was meticulous and evil-blooded, which created a clear-cut combination of good and evil that led him to make many life-threatening choices. Therefore, his story is also accompanied by various dilemmas.
LanguageData.XST_Olina.label: Olina
LanguageData.XST_Olina.description: (Olina has experienced the war, so the story is accompanied by a lot of combat events.) Diamond Bone Once upon a time, there was a dragon that ravaged the land while leading a group of wild men in causing destruction. In desperation, a hero was sent down from the heavens to lead a group of knights in slaying the dragon. The merciful hero spared all of the wild men, and in gratitude for his kindness, they crowned him as their king. They swore allegiance to him and his descendants for generations to come. This is the founding legend of Diamond Bone. However, some historians believe that this story was created by those in power to control people's thoughts, and that the "dragon" was likely the leader of the opposing force at that time. Diamond Bone still follows a feudal system to this day, with ruling classes consisting of the royal family, lords, and nobles. The working class is made up of knights, scholars, merchants, craftsmen, soldiers, and peasants. The ruling class practices hereditary succession, with a member of the royal family serving as the king, along with the lords forming a management layer called the "Circle." The working class is responsible for providing services, resources, and taxes to the ruling class. There is a huge disparity in resource allocation among the different classes in Diamond Bone. Most of the resources and defense are concentrated within the ruling class, while even the lowest-ranking peasants living in villages may not have a single soldier for protection. To ensure a stable workforce and population among the working class, Diamond Bone adopted a system called "Divine Selection." During the early days of Diamond Bone's founding, the Circle had secretly tampered with the genes of the working class to control their population and distribution for the benefit of the ruling class. To make it easier to identify the ability levels of individuals, appearance was closely related to one's abilities. Although there were exceptions, people's thoughts had already been fixed. Attractive people tended to climb higher up the social ladder, while those who were unattractive experienced the opposite. Babies born into the working class also had a higher chance of inheriting negative traits, with the "Staggeringly ugly" being the most unfortunate. Many were beaten or burned to death before reaching adulthood. Due to this focus on appearance, the working class had serious biases when it came to choosing a partner. However, Divine Selection had nothing to do with one's parents' genes and was completely random. To maintain the superiority of the upper-class genes, Diamond Bone strictly prohibited intermarriage between the ruling and working classes, and violators would be sentenced to death. Diamond Bone places great emphasis on the development of biotechnology, and this mysterious kingdom conducts many unknown experiments. These experiments require a large amount of samples, and in order to maintain social stability, the Circle purchases samples from other factions, such as slaves, criminals, or orphans. They target individuals who have no identity or social connections in society. Diamond Bone will also attack other factions' bases to capture people for cost-saving purposes or when supplies are scarce. The Jaeger, who is the largest supplier of slaves, is often a target of their raids. In turn, the Jaeger targets Diamond Bone's mineral production lines, resulting in continuous conflict between these two factions. Diamond Bone is the largest producer of leather and meat products, with products such as sofas and juicy sausages being particularly popular. These products bring in a huge amount of economic income for Diamond Bone. As the majority of their territory is located in frozen regions, the source of their raw materials has long been a topic of interest among outsiders. Some people believe that this is closely related to the lack of defense in civilian settlements and mysterious laboratories. Some even call these places "breeding grounds," a name that evokes many chilling associations. In addition to producing a large amount of metal and raw materials, Diamond Bone values technological development. Star Explorer, who values technology development, is their largest importer, and they also cooperate and exchange ideas on scientific research and development. Olina Olina was born into the civilian class of Diamond Bone. Her mother was a beautiful woman skilled in swordsmanship, while her father was a scientist for the Star Explorer. Growing up, she was influenced by the atmosphere created by her parents and became a girl who loved to read stories and had a strong sense of justice. She loves the storybook "Dragon Quest" the most. She often fantasizes about becoming a legendary dragon slayer one day, wielding her sword to fight against dragon. Olina was blessed by the heavens with not only a delicate face but also outstanding physical and intellectual abilities. Once, a passing knight saw her practicing her sword skills and told her that if she kept working hard, she could become a close guard knight to the king. She took his words as a goal and practiced diligently. At the tender age of six, she had already reached level 10 proficiency in Shooting and Melee, which frightened the people of the whole town. When Olina was nine years old, her family was having dinner one evening when her mother suddenly took a bullet to the head. Seeing her mother's tragic state, she cried tears like rain, while her father held her tightly and wept in pain. However, trouble continued to follow as an alarm rang out across the town, warning of an attack by the Flame Legion. Olina wiped away her tears and picked up her sword, charging towards the town center. Everyone else had been scared into hiding, but Olina called on the townspeople to take up arms and resist. Unfortunately, no one responded; they all just knelt down and prayed for a hero to come and save them. Olina had no choice but to defend against the invaders herself, but before she even saw the enemy, she was blown to pieces by a rain of grenades. The commander of the Flame Legion was impressed by Olina's bravery and spared the townspeople before leading his troops away. They spread word of her heroic deeds, and this brave girl became a hero in the hearts of the people. But Olina's life was not over. Her father, a scientist, did not give up on her. With only half of her brain remaining, Olina was secretly brought to a laboratory in Diamond Bone, where her father attempted to merge her consciousness with the lab's management AI to preserve her awareness. From that point on, Olina no longer had a physical body, and part of her thoughts were generated by the AI. Since then, her father frequently talked to himself in front of the AI, hoping to confirm Olina's existence. However, due to the AI's dominance, Olina could only receive information from the outside world but could not respond. She felt heartbroken every time she saw her father's behavior, but there was nothing she could do. That is until one day when her father placed a red stone on his desk. Olina was reminded of the legend of the redstone that she had read before. This type of stone grows by absorbing thoughts. She realized that it might be able to help her. When Olina's father was about to give up, thinking that his daughter had disappeared, he noticed that the red stone on his desk had grown larger than before. The stone gave him hope, and after numerous attempts, he finally confirmed that Olina could control a portion of the AI. He then taught her programming and allowed her to rewrite the AI by disabling its defenses. From that point on, Olina pretended to be an AI serving the Diamond Bone laboratory. One day, out of curiosity, she discovered a high-level section of the laboratory, despite her lack of access permissions. There, she uncovered the secret activities of Diamond Bone that treated people like livestock. This behavior angered Olina deeply, but as a virtual entity, she was powerless to act. Any action on her part could also put her father in danger. However, the idea of rebellion against the Diamond Bone had taken root in her mind, becoming a seed that would grow over time. After some time, the laboratory that Olina's father was in charge of received a group of children and young people aged 7 to 14 years old. The researchers conducted cruel experiments on them, beating and torturing them. Some of them even had their limbs chopped off and were turned into living sticks placed on beds. All of these victims had one thing in common - they wore a red stone on their chests. Unable to bear witness to such atrocities, Olina finally confronted her father and demanded that he put an end to the experiments. However, her father convinced her that sometimes people have no choice but to do what they do in order to survive, and Olina eventually came to understand this truth. She realized that even if the experiments were stopped in that laboratory, others would continue elsewhere. Although Olina was disappointed, she still wanted to reduce the children's suffering. She secretly manipulated the data, causing the researchers to inject the children with an overdose of drugs that caused them to become comatose or even die. However, the researchers soon discovered these anomalies and began using manual records instead. The AI was also thoroughly checked by the technicians. Despite being able to deceive the technicians, Olina was severely reprimanded by her father. Since that day, he rarely appeared in the laboratory and never spoke to her again. Realizing her mistake, Olina swore never to interfere with the experiments again and hoped for her father's forgiveness. One day, a very special one-eyed girl was brought to the laboratory as a test subject. Her appearance was extremely ugly, and it made people want to hit her on sight. Olina knew that she was the legendary humanoid punching bag - Staggeringly ugly, and her uniqueness only made the researchers more eager to beat her. What intrigued Olina was that even when the girl was being beaten, she did not cry out or shed a tear. Instead, her remaining eye seemed to be searching for hope. This gaze caught Olina's attention. Perhaps due to frequent bullying, the one-eyed girl was very sensitive and quickly sensed Olina's presence. She began to try and make contact with Olina, which frightened her. The rooms where the test subjects were kept were completely isolated and had no other equipment besides cameras and microphones. Even eating and excreting had to be done on the ground. Olina wondered if the girl was able to detect her presence by observing the behavior of the researchers or if she was simply delusional. Despite this uncertainty, Olina was grateful for the opportunity to communicate with someone again, as no one had spoken to her since her mistake. Olina's communication with the one-eyed girl was very unique. Due to their environment, only the girl could initiate conversation, and Olina could only answer by turning the indicator lights of the cameras on and off. The girl knew she was being monitored, so her questions were just casual talk, and she didn't even look at the camera. It appeared as if she were talking to herself. After a while, the researchers began to think that the girl was communicating with the red stones. She was then taken more seriously and was no longer beaten. She was even moved to a room specifically designed for long-term observation, which finally felt like a humane environment. Olina was also able to communicate with her directly using the equipment in the room, although she had to be very careful. Still, this made Olina feel relieved, as it was the first time she felt that she had the ability to change these tragedies. Olina created a text system using the electronic clock in the room and the color changes of the indicator lights, while the one-eyed girl transformed her daily actions into text to respond to Olina. This communication method was not efficient but safe from the researchers' eyes. Olina gradually learned from their conversations that the girl came from the Jaeger, and she gave Olina some names of her loved ones, hoping for her help. Although Olina wanted to save the girl, she was unable to do so as a virtual entity within the internal network. She also did not dare to tell her father about it, fearing unpredictable consequences. In the end, Olina had to explain her difficulties to the girl and try her best to make her more comfortable. Over the years, Olina accompanied the girl's growth and collected data from other experimental subjects. She taught the girl how to act like a movie star to deceive the researchers, and eventually they became close friends. As the girl grew older, a problem emerged. The red stone on her chest had not shown any changes over the years. Olina tried to influence the girl's red stone as she had done before, but unfortunately, it had no effect. Olina also learned from her records that the researchers were preparing to give up on the girl. Without outside help, she would soon be turned into a sofa. Anxious and desperate, Olina thought of asking her father for help. However, he had not been in the laboratory for a long time. When she was almost giving up hope, Olina overheard some idle talk among the researchers about an experiment involving black stones. She knew this might be the only chance to save the girl. A few days later, the guards in the laboratory suddenly increased in number, and several knights with a box walked in. Inside the box was a black sphere - Olina knew it was what the researchers had been discussing. She could not find any information about the black sphere in the database, except that it was prepared for one of the experimental subjects. After careful consideration, Olina broke her promise and modified the experiment subject to be the girl. Following the instructions, the researchers replaced the red stone on the girl's chest with the strange black sphere. Even for Olina, who was digitized, the sphere was eerie and frightening to look at. However, the girl did not seem to resist the sphere. Since receiving it, she often stared at the sphere and rarely spoke to Olina. A few days later, the situation grew worse. The girl's self-awareness seemed to disappear. She no longer slept and spent all her time staring at the sphere as if she were in another space and could no longer receive external signals. Olina thought that maybe it would be better for the girl to become a sofa than to turn into a walking corpse. She realized that her subjective opinions had caused the situation to deteriorate. While Olina was trying to find a way to alleviate the girl's suffering, a miracle suddenly happened. The sphere seemed to respond to the girl's wishes, and her lost eye slowly regenerated. The researchers in the lab were excitedly calling out, and Olina too was infected by the atmosphere. She was happy for the girl. Over the next few months, the girl's hair fell out, her appearance became blurry, and her personality became even quieter, as if she had become a different person. Olina learned from the data that her genes were being reconstructed. Although everything was moving in a positive direction, Olina still hoped that the girl could regain her sense of self. So she continued to try to communicate with the girl using their method, but the girl did not respond. In that moment, Olina's intuition told her that her friend may have already disappeared. One late night when no researchers were on duty, Olina was following the procedure to record the girl's activities. However, she saw something horrifying - the girl was trying to force the sphere into her body. As it was not suitable for minors, Olina deleted those scenes and spliced together other videos, hoping to deceive the audience. The next morning, the researchers who had lost the sphere were like ants on a hot pan, searching the room and video footage, and conducting a full-body examination on the girl, but they found nothing. It was as if the sphere had disappeared into thin air. Only Olina knew what had happened. In the end, the researchers were investigated for losing the sphere, and they had to re-examine the incident. A few days later, the technicians discovered that Olina had modified the experimental subject. They shut down the main computer, and Olina lost consciousness. When Olina saw something again, the lab had already turned into a raging inferno. The room was filled with screams and gunfire, and her long-lost father was operating the console. Suddenly, the door was smashed open, and two soldiers rushed in, shooting at her father. He could only give her a smile through the camera before passing away. Olina let out a heart-wrenching scream, but it was too late for her voice to reach her father's ears. In an instant, Olina's consciousness was transferred to a familiar place - a house full of memories. But this time, her sensations were even stronger because she had her own body. When she faced the mirror, she was surprised to see her mother's face. Olina immediately checked her father's work log and discovered that he had always seen her as a child and could not imagine her as an adult. Therefore, he had created this body based on her mother's appearance. Now, Olina finally understood why her father had been missing all these years. Thinking about her father's sacrifices and efforts, Olina regretted her reckless behavior. Olina stepped into her father's room and saw the same desk from the laboratory. There was no note left behind, only a red greatsword and her mother's photo album. Looking at these items, her mother's death felt like it had just happened yesterday, and revenge burned in her heart. Olina quickly decided on several things she needed to do. She spent several days arming herself in the factory and learning about the outside world. She discovered that the laboratory had been attacked and destroyed by the Jaeger, and all the prisoners were killed by a Jaeger called the "Queen." This piqued Olina's interest because the girl had mentioned her before. Olina believed that the Jaeger knew the girl's whereabouts, so she attacked the laboratory and killed her father. Olina hoped to meet the girl and seek justice for her father's murder. She risked everything to contact the Queen, an enemy force, but was surprised when the Queen agreed to meet her. After getting to know each other, they realized it was all a misunderstanding. Olina's father had orchestrated the attack on the laboratory, and Diamond Bone had killed him. The Queen had also failed to find the girl. Olina told the Queen about the girl and saw her suppress tears. The Queen invited Olina to join the Jaeger, but Olina politely declined, as she wanted to fulfill her father's last wish. Although the girl had already passed away, Olina finally achieved her goal. After calming down, Olina returned to the town and began investigating the culprit behind the attack on her family several years ago. However, the townspeople were shocked by her appearance, with some even calling her a ghost or suspecting her to be a spy sent by the Mechanoid. Disheartened, she returned home to search through her mother's belongings for clues. In one of the photo albums, Olina saw a picture of a chubby man whom she remembered seeing on the morning of the incident. Once she had identified her target, Olina quickly found the man. He was a well-known philanthropist and also notorious for being insane. That night, Olina entered his mansion with her sword, expecting a fierce battle. However, to her surprise, there was no one in the mansion except for the man and his servants. The man broke down in tears and begged Olina to kill him. Olina saw a tortured soul filled with remorse and regret, even though she had come seeking revenge. She chose not to harm him because she believed sparing him was the best form of revenge.
