After experiencing all these events, Olina had resolved all her issues and decided to give up everything and live a normal life as a technician. Unfortunately, a few days later, a group of Diamond Bone knights appeared in the town. They were heavily armed and clearly not there for a casual visit. The frightened townspeople immediately thought of the strange android girl and tried to reveal Olina's whereabouts to the knights. But they were mistaken; the knights had come to slaughter the village. Those few townspeople who attempted to speak were beheaded without a word. Olina was in the workshop when she heard the sounds of panic and screams, so she grabbed her sword and rushed outside. The knights were surprised to see Olina. The fact that someone in an ordinary small town possesses a redstone weapon is an extremely unusual occurrence. They temporarily halted their attack. The frightened townspeople gathered behind Olina. Seeing that there were over ten knights, Olina knew she couldn't win and proposed the knight's protocol for a one-on-one battle. The knights looked at a young black knight who was waiting for his decision. Olina knew he was the leader of the knights. However, upon looking at her entire advanced biomechanical body, the knight captain launched a full-scale attack. Olina had no choice but to use her sword to stop them. However, there were too many knights, and the townspeople behind her were being slaughtered. She shouted to the townspeople to hide in her workshop, then fought and retreated to buy them time. The townspeople rushed inside and closed the door, leaving the knights unable to break through. They focused their attention back on Olina. Despite knowing that she couldn't survive against so many knights, Olina still held her sword tightly and fought bravely to the end. Inside the workshop, the townspeople saw the same little girl who had fought against the Flame Legion years ago. Now that girl had grown up and was still protecting them. They felt ashamed of their previous cowardice and decided not to be weak anymore. They picked up the engineering holy sword in the workshop and stood together with determination. They opened the door, and when the knights saw them, they rushed in to collect heads. However, as soon as one of the knights stepped into the entrance, he suffered over thirty blows and fainted. The other knights were shocked and the knight captain immediately ordered the attack to stop and focused on dealing with the android girl. Olina had sustained several wounds, but her face displayed an excited smile as there were only eight opponents left. In response, the knight captain ordered the remaining knights to retrieve their guns and avoid further casualties. The townspeople attempted to assist, but Olina directed them to escape through the workshop's rear door. With the knights retrieving their firearms, the townspeople knew they could no longer be of assistance and followed Olina's instructions to flee. They watched in despair as the situation unfolded before them, hoping that Olina would prevail against all odds. Olina was not intimidated by the knights' firearms. Braving through the conflict, she charged forward and swiftly dispatched two of the gun-wielding knights with her sword. The others were so frightened that they considered retrieving their swords. The knight captain ordered them to remain calm and scattered them to surround Olina. He drew his sword, engaged Olina in battle, and realized that he was outmatched after a few strikes. As a result, he retreated and commanded the knights to concentrate on shooting at Olina's legs. With several shots fired, one of Olina's legs was blown off, causing her to stumble. The knights then swarmed Olina, taking advantage of her weakened state. Despite her stumbling movements and injuries, Olina fought back with all her might. Unfortunately, she was hit multiple times with bullets and the knights eventually broke both of her hands. She fell to the ground, waiting for death to arrive. The knights began to mistreat her out of frustration, but the knight captain admired Olina's courage and believed that a warrior like her deserved respect. The knight captain approached the other knights and put a stop to their mistreatment of Olina. He then pulled out his gun and shot her in the head six times at close range. Only one bullet hit its mark, tearing off the left side of Olina's face, including her eye. The surrounding knights laughed at the black knight's poor shooting skill and ridiculed him for relying solely on his sword. Feeling ashamed, the knight captain unsheathed his sword and prepared to deliver the final blow by cutting Olina in half. However, he suddenly heard gunshots in the distance, causing the knights to become disoriented. Soon after, the townspeople arrived with a team of Jaegers, charging into the area and forcing the knights to surrender. The knight captain removed his mask, revealing a face full of indignation. At this moment, Olina saw the knight captain's face and was filled with a mixture of surprise and joy because he was someone she knew. After the situation was brought under control by the Jaegers, a chubby man slowly walked towards them - her mother's killer. Olina was stunned as she watched him, and he ordered his troops to take all the townspeople and prisoners back with the Jaegers. On their journey, he informed Olina that he had received intelligence that Diamond Bone was cleaning up the small town. Worried, he hired the Jaegers to come to their aid. However, Olina harbored deep hatred towards the man and didn't even look at him throughout the entire journey. Upon returning to the Jaeger city, Olina met the queen again. The queen arranged accommodations for them and promised to let the townspeople decide whether to stay or leave. Olina was perplexed by the queen's actions because they were not something she would expect from a Jaeger she knew. Nevertheless, she genuinely appreciated the queen's kindness. During her time with the Jaeger, the Chubby Man rented a workspace to repair Olina. She knew that he was doing this to make up for his past mistakes and therefore did not want to give him the opportunity. She refused to let him repair her, and even spoke harshly to him. However, the Chubby Man never complained and remained determined to fix her. Olina was not a cruel person, and eventually allowed him to help her assemble herself. It was then that she realized that the Chubby Man's understanding of cybernetics and innovation far surpassed that of his father. He helped her rebuild her neural network and synthetic skin, allowing Olina to feel like a human rather than a machine once again. However, this also resulted in her having to readapt to her body and caused severe discomfort. Throughout the month, the Chubby Man was always by her side, taking care of her. Olina began to realize that he was an extremely caring and gentle person, and could not understand why he had killed her mother. With the Chubby Man's meticulous care, her mindset gradually changed, and she came to know that he had been living in pain for many years, and that his punishment had been enough. As a result, she forgave him and developed another strange feeling towards him. Every time she looked at his cybernetic heart, her heart would pound like a deer in headlights. However, Olina still could not let go of her mother's death, which made her feel very scared. To control these emotions, Olina only allowed the Chubby Man to use old mechanical parts to repair her left eye and face, as a reminder of how her mother died. While repairing Olina's head, the Chubby Man discovered a hidden memory storage that was programmed to activate automatically after she received her body. However, due to incomplete equipment, it was not triggered. The Chubby Man did not peek and instead handed it over to Olina when she woke up. Nervously, she waited until the Chubby Man left before opening the memory storage to browse its contents. She discovered that it contained her father's final words. Her father revealed that he was still working for the Star Explorer and had participated in a special forces research project. Joining Diamond Bone was only a means to steal genetic technology. It wasn't until he met Olina's mother that he learned about the terrifying rule of the Circle of Diamond Bone. As a result of his unique identity, any changes could lead to suspicion, so he remained indifferent to those things. However, Olina changed his mind. Her father said that she inherited her mother's personality, and after experiencing what happened in the laboratory, he knew that she would inevitably cause trouble. That's why he built this body for her, allowing her to make her own decisions. Attached to the message were piles of experimental data from the Circle and a Dragon Quest e-book. Upon learning everything, Olina felt very distressed, and promised her father that she would use this body well. After Olina was repaired, she began discussing the matter with Chubby Man. She only wanted to put an end to Diamond Bone's twisted experiments and flesh production line, but without inciting a war. However, Chubby Man reminded her that the Circle wouldn't easily relinquish their power. If she chose that path, war would be inevitable. Realizing that she had no military power, Olina felt helpless. Seeing her distressed, Chubby Man advised her to pay attention to the strange behavior of the Jaegers, as they might provide some insight. Having been inspired, Olina began recalling the Jaegers and realized that there were many inconsistencies, especially with the person who called herself the Queen. She didn't seem to be a typical sniper but rather more like a commander. Olina started analyzing Queen's past achievements and the history of the Jaegers, and the conclusion she drew left her stunned. Perhaps she was in the midst of the Righteous League. Knowing it was dangerous, Olina didn't want to implicate Chubby Man, so she quietly left while he slept. After formulating her plan, Olina immediately went to meet with the knights who had attacked the small town. They weren't being held captive but were instead living together in one room. Using the Queen's name, Olina greeted the guard at the door and was allowed entry. After speaking with the knights, Olina learned that the Circle had ordered the purging of all those associated with the laboratory - a direct violation of the knight's code. However, as soldiers, they were obligated to follow orders. Olina revealed her own experiences and the secrets of the Circle to the knights, leaving them shocked and angry. They were also worried about the safety of their families because the Circle would turn them into sofas if their captives didn't take their own lives to prevent information from leaking out. Seeing the dejected knights, Olina outlined her plan and promised to first try to secure their families' safety. After much consideration, the knights decided to take a gamble and pledged loyalty to Olina. At this point, mischievous Olina looked towards the knight captain and asked him if he remembered her. The bewildered knight captain studied Olina's face closely and suddenly remembered the young girl who used to play with swords. With a grin, Olina pointed to her left eye and said that the knight captain's shooting skill had saved her life. The knight captain blushed, and the entire pledge ceremony became less serious after that playful moment. Olina knew that only six knights could not possibly fight against the whole country, so she decided to trust her instincts and take a gamble. This was the key to the plan. Olina met with the queen again and told her everything she knew about the Circle and her ideas. As expected, the sniper was not an ordinary person. Soon after, Jaeger's military government sent several troops to support them. They disguised themselves as a merchant caravan and split into six groups. They successfully rescued the knights' families, and the knights then succeeded in turning some of the Knight Orders against the Circle. Several small towns broke free from the Circle's control. However, what surprised Olina was that the Circle's fear and domination were like a bomb, waiting for someone to light the fuse. After their deeds became known, many people imitated Olina's actions, and Diamond Bone suddenly flared up, causing the country to face division. Olina was further demonized by the Circle as the legendary "Dragon", accused of joining hostile forces to invade the country. However, the war did not last long. The unrest affected the supply of resources from other countries, and six months later, the Star Explorer intervened in the situation. They supported the weak Circle troops and resisted the rebels' attacks. The Star Explorer then negotiated with the Circle to halt all human experiments and allow the rebels to become independent as conditions for a ceasefire agreement. Peace was restored once again. After everything returned to calm, the knight captain saw Olina alone and lonely, he invited her to live with him and his family. Olina was envious of their happy life but felt lost when she thought about her own background. Seeing this, the knight captain began to provide her with psychological counseling and advised her to follow her heart instead of being bound by the past. It was then that Olina remembered the chubby man. After considering it for a few days, Olina resolutely let go of her past and embarked on a journey once again to find the chubby man. *Olina is a mischievous little devil who has experienced the weight of war, so her story carries a heavy burden. Please prepare yourself mentally.
LanguageData.XST_XvA.label: XvA
LanguageData.XST_XvA.description: (XvA's brain is a bit strange, it can be very stable in her story, and it can be a nightmare.) XEO The enigmatic assassination organization has a name that has no particular meaning when viewed as words. It is said to be an ancient pictographic symbol representing a poisonous plant. The founding members of the organization often used this plant to assassinate those who abused their power, which led to the organization being named after it. XEO has no formal social structure, and assassins are typically ordinary civilians or nobles living among various factions. They regularly provide funds to maintain the organization's operations. The Elder Synod, led by four elders, manages the organization. Each elder serves a four-year term, with one member leaving and a new elder joining each year. The primary duties of the Elder Synod include collecting and organizing intelligence and deciding which assassins will execute missions. If the organization encounters significant problems, they make the final decision. Elite assassins known as Templar Musha protect the Elder Synod. However, due to the passage of time and concentration of power, the Elder Synod has become corrupt. There are rumors that anyone can avoid being assassinated if they pay enough money, while those who are killed did not pay enough. Some even believe that they accept money in exchange for killing people. XEO's mission is to maintain balance, and they ignore laws and conventions. They target those who abuse their power by carrying out assassinations, and also engage in acts of robbing the rich to help the poor or assisting local organizations in resisting invading forces. Many people see them as righteous heroes, but most of their targets come from the Star Explorers, leading to the Star Explorer Council employing Jaegers to launch a sustained campaign against them. However, their assassinations of Star Explorers have not stopped. XEO's unique weapon, the High Freguency Blade, is made of Song Crystals. Legend has it that these crystals undergo a supersonic reaction when influenced by song, producing an incredible destructive force. Although this is just a rumor, there are indeed some talented singers within the organization who regularly hold concerts to raise funds and recruit new members. XEO also places great emphasis on archaeological research, believing that through archaeology, they can discover the origin of the world. It is said that they were once inspired by an artifact from a lost civilization, which led them to believe that a black apple with a bite taken out of it is related to the Creator, and have been searching for this apple ever since. XEO follows a ritual called the "Knell of Death", where they fire a warning shot before carrying out an assassination to give the target a chance to survive. If the target manages to kill the assassin, the organization will not carry out another assassination against them for four years, after which they will be reevaluated. XEO views this as a chance for the Creator to give the target an opportunity to reform. However, this overt ritual has led to some assassins being caught by the target's guards and bound for the target to execute them. As a result, many assassins within the organization doubt the necessity of this ritual. XvA During a mission to liberate a Diamond Bone's laboratory, XEO was ambushed by Jaegers, resulting in heavy casualties. One of the injured assassins was rescued by a girl who had no place to go. After recovering from her injuries, the assassin brought her back to the organization. With no memories of her past, she looked different from humans and had great plasticity in her personality, leading the organization to believe that she was something that escaped from the laboratory. Since she had no name, the Elders gave her the name "XvA", meaning to spread the voice of the organization. They then decided to train her as an idol like figure, and entrusted her to a retired master assassin for training. The Master Assassin was a lazybone, leading a leisurely life until he was assigned a mission. He noticed that XvA looked attractive and thought it would be nice to keep her as a decoration, so he took on the task. However, he later discovered that this seemingly dull girl actually had ADHD and an incredible appetite, having eaten up all his supplies within a few days, forcing him to frequently go out to restock. In short, she was a big trouble to him, and he had no intention of teaching her. The Master lived in an island nation that revered martial arts, which produced many internationally renowned action movies. He was careless and only played action movies for her every day. But soon enough, XvA became proficient in combat to the point where her skill level was off the charts, which made the Master uneasy. He was afraid that the Elder Synod would assign more newbies to him due to her impressive progress. His lazy nature began to take over, and he decided to shoot XvA with a rifle, treating it as training while disposing of this gluttonous girl at the same time. Not long after, he tricked XvA into believing that assassins were capable of deflecting bullets with their blades, and convinced her to undergo bullet deflection training. The Master thought that his suspicious reasoning wouldn't convince her, but instead, XvA simply asked him some questions. At that moment, the evil Master was secretly laughing, knowing that this cute and curious girl would soon be shot to pieces like a watermelon. The master explained a lot of nonsense to XvA, talking about attack angles and hit thresholds. After finishing, he called for XvA to stand in position. He solemnly mourned for her for a few seconds, and then fired a shot at her face with his Red Line rifle. The master expected to see XvA explode like a watermelon, but to his surprise, XvA deflected the bullet with inhuman speed. The master was stunned and thought it was just a coincidence. He then fired several more shots, but each one was deflected by XvA. The amazed master raised his thumb and praised her for doing a good job. XvA was overjoyed to receive such praise. Seeing that XvA was skilled at deflecting bullets, the master took out his minigun and tried to burst her high-frequency blade directly, hoping to blow her to pieces. However, after a round of gunfire, the master softened. Not only were all the bullets deflected, but the high-frequency blade did not explode even after multiple strikes. This irrational phenomenon aroused the master's curiosity, and he changed his mindset from murder to research. He even tried using a chain shotgun, but was shocked by the results. The master noticed that XvA had a reflexive action when it came to high-speed flying objects. The higher the rate of fire of the weapon, the faster her reflexes became. After careful observation, he realized that it wasn't because she was fast with the blade, but because the bullets were slowing down. These high-speed flying objects triggered an unknown reactive force field around her, causing a time dilation effect to occur near her body. In fact, she was just playing Fruit Ninja. The high-frequency blade didn't explode because of this effect. The master believed that XvA might be a BUG and became very interested in her. He felt like he had regained his position as a mentor and was cured of his laziness. The master conducted a deeper study on XvA and discovered that her enormous appetite was related to her incredible physical exertion. By controlling her activity level, her food intake could be reduced accordingly. The master felt grateful for this discovery. Furthermore, when he witnessed XvA using her voice to trigger Overfrequency of the high-frequency blade, he believed she was a precious gift from heaven and decided to further explore her abilities. However, the master encountered a problem. He discovered that XvA, who suffered from ADHD, did not know how to use a gun properly. Whenever she picked up a gun, she would start shooting without aiming or even raising the gun, and almost hit the master's knee once. Since then, the scared master had prohibited her from using guns and focused on training her melee combat skills. However, a bigger problem arose when XvA encountered fast-moving objects. She would instinctively slash them with her blade instead of evading them, despite the master's patient explanations. This habit posed a danger, as she might cut down shells or rockets flying toward her. In desperation, the master sternly warned her that excessive use of the high-frequency blade would cause an explosion. Despite his warning, XvA persisted in her ways. The once-calm master lost his temper and scolded XvA, but seeing her pressing her hands against her head in distress, he realized she was doing her best and might not want to behave like this. The master suspected that XvA may have suffered a head injury as a child, as she exhibited loss of memory and lack of critical thinking skills. Such an assassin would be no different from a suicidal agent in executing missions. Master is actually a person with a story. His former assassin girlfriend was also a fool. Master never allowed her to carry out missions alone because he thought she was too foolish. However, one day, he was convinced by her and allowed her to work alone. Unfortunately, it led to a big mistake. While carrying out the "Knell of Death," a bullet hit the bell tower's large bell, alerting their target, and ultimately causing his girlfriend to fall to her death. Master felt extremely guilty about her death, which eventually led to his depression. As a result, he turned into a shut-in and stopped caring about XEO's affairs. Even when offered missions, he would refuse them multiple times. Due to his high status and achievements, the Elder Synod had no choice but to let him retire and live a leisurely life. At this point, the foolish yet cute XvA gives Master a sense of deja vu, and he hopes to make up for his past carelessness through her. He secretly modifies her Blade Armor and high-frequency blade and even passes on his unique assassination technique - "Transport pod drop onto face," to her. In the following month, Master not only tolerated XvA's foolishness but also began to see her as a beloved disciple due to her obedient and docile personality. In an effort to improve her slow-wittedness, Master showed her many romance movies to train her critical thinking skills. However, to his frustration, it seemed that she could only imitate, lacking any understanding of the underlying principles. Soon after, the Elder Synod ordered her to carry out missions, which Master believed was premature. Despite his objections, the Elder Synod insisted and assigned her tasks that were unpopular with other assassins. Worried about XvA and fearful of history repeating itself, Master analyzed and meticulously planned each assassination step for her. Fortunately, XvA did not disappoint him. She followed all of Master's teachings and successfully completed every mission without fail. Oblivious to the nature of the assignments, she killed men, women, and children indiscriminately. Her ghost-like attack style and striking red hair earned her the nickname "Red Scorpion" among witnesses, causing panic among the elites. However, her immoral assignments led to misunderstandings among the organization's members who believed she was a bloodthirsty psychopath. Nevertheless, the Elder Synod gave her high praise as this was exactly the brainless killer they wanted. As XvA's reputation grows higher, troubles come along with it. Despite her lack of contact with anyone within the organization except for the Master, XvA's beauty and unbeatable record have brought her a large number of supporters. However, this rising star has gradually aroused suspicion from the Elder Synod who believes that the Assassin Master has ruined the Idol Master. Furthermore, XvA only accepts missions without communicating with the Elder Synod, causing suspicion to soon turn into fear. They believe that it is necessary to take action to reduce XvA's influence within the organization. The Elder Synod quickly assigned impossible missions to XvA, hoping to make her experience failure and break those legends. However, to their surprise, XvA was able to complete every task they gave her. The Elder Synod's actions only made her reputation spread even faster. Annoyed, the elders began sending spies to monitor her, only to discover that the Master was secretly planning tasks for XvA. She was just responsible for executing them. The paranoid elders believed that the Master was deliberately sabotaging their plans and, in a fit of rage, sent multiple Templar Mushas to assassinate him. Not long after, the Master told XvA that he was going to meeting the Creator and asked her to take care of herself. Naive XvA thought that the Master had built a spaceship and was leaving the planet. She wanted to express her nostalgia and decided to imitate what she had seen in romance movies. XvA cut off a bundle of her red hair and gave it to the Master, telling him not to forget her. After accepting her hair, the Master smiled and patted her head, repeatedly warning her not to perform the "Knell of Death" during assassinations, fearing that it would alert the targets and put her life in danger. XvA did not question why and just followed the Master's words. After losing the Master, XvA became very aggressive when executing her missions. She violated the rules of XEO repeatedly by "Transport pod drop onto face" without issuing "Knell of Death" before assassinating her targets. However, due to her reputation within the organization and the fact that she completed her tasks while greatly increasing XEO's influence, the Elder Synod had no choice but to tolerate her behavior. Some time later, the powerful figures, fearing for their safety, spent a large amount of money hiring Jaegers as security personnel, making assassinations extremely difficult. During this time, XvA's uncensored videos were leaked, and some people who had seen her alluring and seductive performance in "Red Scorpion" became obsessed with her and were willing to spend huge amounts of money to capture her. What was once a hunter became the prey of the rich overnight, and the situation spiraled out of control. Despite the perilous situation, the Elder Synod not only failed to hide XvA but also gave her even more dangerous missions. Rumors spread throughout the organization that the Elder Synod deliberately leaked her videos or even sold her out for money. These rumors eventually reached XvA's ears. Since the chatty Master ascended, no one had spoken to her again. XvA was not used to this and began talking to herself in her mind. The Master had told her that it was thinking, and she started to adapt to this method of communication. However, as XvA learned to think for herself, she began to question her tasks and grow increasingly dissatisfied with the unfair treatment from the Elder Synod. Finally, during a assassination mission, XvA encountered a target who was protecting a child with all of their might. This heroic act made XvA hesitate to carry out the task. She felt a sense of familiarity and eventually decided to spare the target and leave. When news of this spread among the assassins, they were all shocked because it was her first failed mission. Many believed that the Elder Synod would take action over this, and everyone was eagerly waiting for what would happen next. However, to everyone's surprise, the Elder Synod did not take any action. They were actually very pleased because they had finally broken the legend of her invincibility after putting so much effort into doing so. After some time, XvA began to interact with other assassins within the organization and even participated in their missions. However, her habit of "Transport pod drop onto face" caused many problems for the other assassins she worked with. But those who were helped by her sudden attacks were grateful for her aid. As time went on, the other assassins began to understand that XvA was not cold-blooded but rather too simple-minded. Some of them even felt sorry for her and formed a Fan club to teach her how to plan and execute missions better. The assassin who was saved by her became the leader of the club. This initiative was also supported by the Elder Synod because it helped solve the problem of XvA's indiscriminate use of Transport pods. Originally, everything was peaceful and quiet. However, as communication increased between XvA and the organization, many assassins began to follow her lead and stop giving warning before carrying out their assassinations. Soon, this behavior spread like a virus throughout the organization, creating a culture of not sounding the "Knell of Death" before assassination. Some assassins started questioning the commands of the Elder Synod due to unreasonable tasks, and some even worshipped XvA as the goddess of assassination. An atmosphere of deification and rebellion began to brew within the organization. The Elders were once again afraid of XvA, as they never expected that the tool of killing they had created would slowly become a threat to the organization's unity. They decided to take immediate action to prevent the sapling from growing into a big tree, in order to avoid the potential crisis of organizational dissolution. After several weeks of this situation, the Elder Synod summoned XvA back for questioning on charges of disobeying orders. When asked about the reason she had let her targets go, XvA wasn't able to come up with a reasonable explanation and simply replied with "Because the plot required it". The four Elders were shocked upon hearing this flippant response as they deemed it as contempt towards the authority of the council. They accused XvA of having impure thoughts and subsequently ordered her arrest. After four days, she was publicly executed through the cruel punishment known as "Digging Treasure". When the sentence was handed down, even members within the Elder Synod were astonished by the brutality of the punishment. The "Digging Treasure" execution method was notoriously gruesome and caused prolonged suffering for the victim. XvA was naive to the consequences of her actions, heard her name mentioned and believed that she was being punished to complete some organizational online shopping tasks within four days. She obediently followed a group of Templar Mushas to the prison cell. It wasn't until the executioner walked in that XvA realized the severity of the situation. The executioner was ordered by the Elders to make XvA suffer as much as possible before her death. He deliberately explained the execution process in great detail, hoping to scare her. However, he overestimated XvA's reaction. Throughout the explanation, XvA appeared confused and perplexed. As someone who had always dealt with instant and straightforward deaths, the various methods of execution were too complicated for her to understand. She struggled to comprehend and asked questions from time to time. The executioner was so frustrated with her earnestness that he left and locked the door behind him. The cell had no facilities except for a few small ventilation holes on the ceiling that allowed some light to trickle in. The situation was dire, and the worst part for XvA was not being able to locate where the food was delivered. The unknown terror caused her to tremble in fear, huddled in a corner of the cell. On the last day, the conditions in the cell had become unbearable. XvA hadn't had any food or water since she was locked up, and the pain of hunger and thirst was spreading throughout her body. She had defecated and urinated on the floor, and the stench was overwhelming. Although she found the environment familiar, she couldn't recall why. On the brink of collapse, XvA began to fidget restlessly with her hair. Suddenly, the memory of her Master's teachings about how meditation could enhance one's mental state came to mind. Trying to calm herself, XvA started to reflect on everything she had done in her life. XvA tried hard to recall everything that had happened in her life, but unfortunately, she couldn't remember anything other than her time in the organization. However, she didn't want her life to end like this, and her strong will to survive drove her to try and escape. She used her fists to pound on the granite door, but it was futile. Her hands were broken in the process. Although XvA still had two legs, she knew that she wouldn't be able to escape if they were broken too. After much thought, she decided to use her head to break open the door. XvA's head proved to be strong enough to break open the door, but her moment of hope was short-lived. The Plasteel wall that the Elders had installed after the executioner left blocked her escape. However, despite facing despair, XvA refused to give up. After much consideration, she decided to try and break through the wall with her head again. Unfortunately, this attempt only resulted in her knocking herself unconscious. That night, XvA was awakened by the sound of the wall being torn down. She saw several assassins working together to free her, including the Fan club leader who had brought her blade and armor. XvA was moved to tears by their dedication. However, as soon as they left the cell, they realized they were surrounded by Templar Mushas, and the four Elders were among them. It turned out that the Elder Synod had launched a purge to prevent the organization from collapsing because of XvA. When the assassins learned that XvA was sentenced to the "Digging Treasure" punishment, they understood what the Elders were implying. This form of execution was designed to destroy XvA's image in the minds of the assassins. The Elders waited four days before carrying out the sentence to give the assassins time to choose which side they wanted to be on. Anyone who chose to stand with XvA would be purged. Unfortunately, only a few were willing to face death under the Elder Synod's overwhelming power. XvA didn't fully understand the situation and looked puzzled while the assassins took out their weapons. The Elders pointed to their Templar Musha and smirked at XvA, whose hands were broken, and her head was bleeding. The first Elder even threatened to remove XvA's arms and legs then turn her into a body pillow. When the assassins heard this insult to their idol, they launched an attack. However, they were vastly outnumbered and outmatched by the Templar Musha, who wore Valkyrie armor and were three times their number. Under the overwhelming gap in strength, the assassins were quickly defeated, and XvA could only cry out for them to stop fighting. At the end, even her beloved Fan club leader was cut in half. Only her torso remained, and she said to XvA, "I think I can still fight..." before passing away. When XvA saw the twisted expression on her Fan club leader's face, she felt fear for the first time and ran towards the edge of the map to escape. XvA had barely taken a few steps when she was subdued by the mushas and forced to kneel on the ground. One of them held a blade to her neck and asked if she had any last words before meeting the Creator. It was only then that XvA realized the true meaning of "meeting the Creator" and knew that her master had met the same fate at the hands of these murderers. Filled with grief and anger, she lashed out at the members of the Elder Synod, but due to her innocence, her vocabulary was limited and her insults sounded like a broken record. Hearing this, the mushas laughed. After being insulted by XvA and experiencing such a disrespectful scene, it made the four Elders seethe with anger. They commanded the mushas to retreat and then slowly approached XvA in a circle. With evil grins, they began removing their armor piece by piece. The mushas cheered loudly at this spectacle, knowing that an exciting brawl was about to begin. Unfortunately, the Mushas's expectations were soon dashed. XvA was only subjected to a brutal beating by the Elders, who punched and kicked her until she curled up in a ball on the ground. However, XvA did not take the beating lying down and retaliated by insulting them back with the words "You all lost face." This further enraged the Elders, and they hit XvA even harder, specifically targeting her face until they were out of breath and had to stop. The first elder, upon seeing XvA lying limp on the ground, was overcome with wicked thoughts and decided to carry out his earlier promise. He drew his blade and aimed it at XvA's thigh, but to his surprise, XvA bravely intercepted the attack with her head rather than become a body pillow. Unfortunately, her head immediately exploded into a pile of pixels, revealing a black stone where her forehead had been cut open. The Elders were frightened and screamed "Black Apple" before hastily retreating. The mushas who witnessed this terrifying spectacle were also stunned. At that moment, the other three Elders' heads exploded, leaving only the first Elder standing. Realizing they were under attack, the Templar Mushas immediately formed a protective circle around the remaining Elder. Suddenly, thick smoke surrounded them, followed by a series of gunshots. The weapon used by the attacker was not simple, as the flashes and sounds were much stronger than those of a typical firearm. This made it difficult for the Templar Musha to determine the enemy's numbers and position. Since their armor was not penetrated, they decided to maintain their formation, prioritizing the protection of the first Elder who had wet himself and reassessing the situation. With nerves stretched tight, they vaguely saw a person with a devilish smile in the firelight, who quickly disappeared into the darkness along with the sound of shells hitting the ground. When everything returned to calm, the Templar Musha realized that the person had used blanks loaded with magnesium powder. Feeling deceived, the warriors immediately turned towards XvA's location, but she had already disappeared. XvA was severely injured, ran away from the map as fast as she could. She found a small stream, drank some water, and then collapsed. The next day, when XvA woke up, she saw a swollen face on the stream's reflection that looked like a pig's head. This pig head made her feel familiar and uneasy at the same time. She also noticed a black stone on her forehead's crack, which reminded her of what her master often said about her having a "stone brain." The more XvA thought about it, the more scared she became. She tried hard to calm herself down and started to treat her wounds. However, since she had broken hands, she couldn't bandage herself. She knew that if she didn't deal with her wounds properly, she would die from a bacterial infection. At this point, XvA was already afraid of death and burst into tears. Her crying attracted a man in black, whom XvA recognized as her target that she had spared before. However, he seemed different now and exuded an evil aura. XvA became even more frightened when she realized this. She turned around and ran as fast as she could, but her injuries slowed her down and she couldn't outrun the man in black. Within moments, he caught up to her and pushed her from behind, causing her to fall to the ground. The man then took her from behind, assuming a classic position that XvA recognized from action movies - Doge Style. She knew that bad things were about to happen. She was in a state of panic and struggled desperately, but with her hands broken, she could only twist her body repeatedly. The man in black who was being rubbed against by her couldn't bear it anymore, so he knocked her unconscious directly. When XvA woke up again, she found herself in a mountain cave. Her wounds had been treated and there was a bag of supplies next to her. She realized that it was all just a misunderstanding. For the next few days, XvA stayed in the cave and was surprised by how quickly she recovered from her injuries. Her hand, which had been broken, could move freely after just a few days and even the terrible wound on her head had healed and her hair had grown back. XvA started to suspect that she might be some kind of biological weapon. The bag of supplies provided her with clues and within it she found her high-frequency blade. This blade made XvA realize that the same person who rescued her from the cell must have been the one who left the supplies for her. XvA was convinced that the man in black knew something and decided to set out to find him once she had fully recovered. *XvA has successfully escaped and has been living a life on the run, being chased by both organizations and mercenaries. As she tries to make sense of these attacks, her simple mind sometimes struggles to keep up, resulting in scary stories flowing out of her magical thought processes at any given moment.
LanguageData.XST_Baron.label: Baron
LanguageData.XST_Baron.description: (Baron’s attitude towards the colony is the same as the original Randy, but relatively stable.) Flame Legion Also known as the FFF Brigade, where Fire stands for anger, Fear represents fear and Free symbolizes freedom. The FFF Brigade originated from a Star Explorer noble who had compassion for those exiled by the Star Explorer Council. He established an organization to support them, but it was eventually disbanded by the Council. The noble and his family were also exiled for treason. Afterwards, this noble became a leader supported by other exiles and united various tribes to form the Flame Legion. The Flame Legion's supreme decision-maker is the Supreme Leader, who is elected by the Corps Leaders and holds the position until death or abdication. The Supreme Leader regularly convenes meetings with the Corps Leaders to decide on internal affairs of the Legion. The Corps Leaders, in turn, are elected by the Commanders and are primarily responsible for managing the troops, with few engaging in pillaging. The majority of troop activities are left to the discretion of individual Commanders, who simply need to report to their Corps Leader periodically. Only those with recruitment rights may gather members to form a troop within the Legion and become Commander. However, Commanders do not have the authority to forcefully recruit members; joining is at the discretion of individual members. Commanders must also regularly lead soldiers on pillage raids, otherwise, soldiers may choose to leave. The structure of the Flame Legion's membership is highly complex, as the punishment of exile often extends to relatives. Within the organization, there are not only criminals and laborers but also scholars and nobles who hold a vast amount of knowledge. In addition, various tribes have come together to form a diverse yet disorganized structure. Despite this, their technology is not outdated, and they share a common belief that justice can be brought about through the power of flames. The Flame Legion's integration of tribal cultures and acceptance of inter-tribal marriage has resulted in the emergence of a disease known as "Bear Grylls Syndrome" within their ranks. Those afflicted with this disease taste chicken in everything they eat, and among them are some people known as "Hannibal" who have committed gruesome acts. Diamond Bone's doctors have studied these patients and discovered that their genes have mutated due to prolonged consumption of chicken. The Flame Legion has repeatedly attacked the Star Explorer's base, prompting them to strengthen their defense and launch targeted strikes against the Flame Legion. As a result, looting became extremely difficult. Soon after, the Flame Legion shifted their focus to the villages of Diamond Bone, which lacked proper defenses. Over the course of a week, six villages were brutally attacked, yet strangely, Diamond Bone did not respond to these actions, thus becoming a long-term "supply point" for the Flame Legion. Baron Baron was born into a craftsman family in Diamond Bone. With his curious mind, he developed a love for technology from a young age. Despite being overweight and unathletic due to his sedentary lifestyle at home, he had a sharp mind and a passion for helping others, which made him well-liked among his peers. After Barron grew up, he developed a strong affection for his childhood friend who was very beautiful. He pursued her ardently but unfortunately, she repeatedly rejected him. Eventually, when his first love found someone else, he left the small town where he had always lived, feeling heartbroken. When Baron was looking for other towns to join in the vicinity, he discovered that the area was already highly developed. As an mix Intellectual skill with dual fire, he was nothing but a waste. Just as he was almost starving to death, he was taken in by a kind Flame Legion commander. The commander saw that Baron was talented and gifted, so he gave him a weapon design magazine and suggested that he pursue a career in the Legion's weapons design industry to showcase his talents once he had mastered the craft. The intelligent Baron mastered the essence of weapon design in just a few days and became the Flame Legion's weapon designer. Over the course of 10 years, he designed practical and easily producible weapons that were widely adopted by many Corps Leaders and received high praise. As a reward, the supreme leader later gave him the recruitment rights. With his reputation soaring, Baron quickly formed his own private army. A few months later, Baron was summoned by the supreme leader and asked to create a guided weapon to counter increasingly advanced defense systems. However, Baron knew that the Flame Legion did not have sufficient resources for the development and production of the necessary components. He then remembered a previous research project involving redstone that had similar principles, and began working on it from that angle. After several months, Baron completed the model of the weapon and released it to the public, calling it an Emotion-Driven Inducer. However, the model quickly became the subject of ridicule by his peers, with some even saying that he was crazy since the weapon lacked any mechanical or firing structure. Despite the mockery, Baron persisted and made many attempts, but no matter what he tried, he could never get the bullets to come out. Eventually, even he began to doubt his own sanity. Baron was at a loss, decided to return to Diamond Bone Town in search of information and visit his parents along the way. However, upon arriving at the town, he learned of a tragic event. It turned out that Baron's unauthorized departure years ago had angered the lord, and as a result, the townspeople killed his entire family. They then slaughtered his family members and presented their remains as a sofa to the lord, in order to calm the situation. It turned out that Baron's unauthorized departure years ago had angered the lord, and as a result, the townspeople killed his entire family. They then slaughtered his family members and presented their remains as a sofa to the lord, in order to calm the situation. Upon hearing of the tragedy, Baron was devastated and was chased out of the town amidst insults from the locals. As he was about to leave, he saw a couple with a little girl, and the mother of the girl - his first love - waved and smiled at him. At that moment, Baron was stunned, his mind blank as if something had hit him, leaving him with a void of emptiness. At that moment, Baron was stunned, his mind blank as if something had hit him, leaving him with a void of emptiness. However, that empty space soon turned into a raging inferno that night. Several hundred meters from the town of Diamond Bone, Baron picked up his experimental rifle and envisioned shooting the woman's face. Amidst a loud shriek, Baron knew his weapon had succeeded. But this excitement could not conceal his anger as he commanded his Flame Legion troops to attack the town. After a round of explosions, the town was taken down. As Baron and his soldiers entered the town for the final kill, he saw the shattered remains of that little girl's body, which weighed heavily on him. In the end, he spared all the townspeople and left with his troops. After returning, Baron released a new weapon with an innovative firing system that could penetrate obstacles and hit targets, making it very popular. He earned a large sum of patent fees and invested wisely, which quickly brought him a continuous flow of wealth. His peers no longer dared to ridicule him, and soldiers spread the story of how he used this weapon to blow off his first love's head. As a result, others called him "Headshot Baron." However, Baron hated this nickname because ever since that day, he couldn't sleep peacefully at night and kept dreaming of his first love and the broken body of the little girl. Regretful and desperate, Baron often returned to the small town and fantasized about starting over, but each time he was confronted with the harsh reality of an empty house. Baron, who had become mentally exhausted, was no longer able to design new weapons. His private army also gradually left him because he stopped carrying out plundering activities. Every night, his dreams and guilt haunted him, driving him to seek redemption. This mindset led Baron to become a good person. He stopped designing weapons and instead focused on studying biomechanics and medicine. Later, he established a hospital that provided free prosthetic services to those in need of them. He even took in slaves who were abandoned and would eventually be used as food, training them and sending them to the upper class society to improve their destinies. As a result, his reputation quickly spread throughout various regions, and people began calling him "Virtuous Doctor" instead of "Headshot Baron". His fulfilling life gradually drove away his nightmares. However, as his sense of justice grew stronger, he openly criticized the Supreme Leader's looting actions towards villages during a banquet. The Supreme Leader, upon learning of this, became furious and withdrew Baron's recruitment rights, assigning him to an active army that required daily participation in looting activities. Although Baron was very unwilling, he had no choice but to accept the order. In the midst of looting and pillaging, Baron insisted on providing only logistical support. Although the commander was very unhappy with him, he couldn't do anything because of his reputation and status, so he had to go along with him. One day, while in charge of communications, Baron received an unknown message that indicated a team of archaeologists was moving near their troops. Seeing the message, the commander became excited and ordered the soldiers to prepare for attack. In the end, they attacked the archaeology team near an ancient site, captured many people, and obtained a large amount of resources. The commander believed that much of it was useless junk and too heavy to carry with the travel party, so he ordered Baron to sort through it. While carefully sorting through the resources, the curious Baron discovered a strange black stone that resembled crystal. He picked it up and examined it closely, only to be amazed that the stone did not form an image reflected by light entering his eyes, but instead appeared directly in his mind. Looking at it felt like gazing into an abyss, giving him chills. He was so frightened that he threw the stone into the pile of resources and continued sorting. After sorting through the supplies, Baron discovered a large number of items with the Star Explorer logo, leading him to suspect that the archaeology team belonged to them. The fact that the message was of unknown origin made him even more uneasy, so he advised the Commander to leave as soon as possible. However, the Commander was already displeased with Baron's tendency to act independently, and now he seemed to want to take over command, despite the late hour. Stubbornly, he insisted on staying nearby and setting up camp out of spite. As a result, the troops were ambushed by an unknown force that night and in the chaos, they had to abandon all their supplies, taking only three prisoners and some food as they escaped. The three prisoners turned out to be a family. At first, the troops only captured the child, but the parents caught up and were also taken into custody. However, the Flame Legion soldiers were not entirely heartless. The Commander promised that as long as the family members reached the safe zone, they would all be released. During this time, the couple shared their knowledge and stories about archaeology with the soldiers, and they were treated with respect and kindness instead of being treated like prisoners. Baron felt a sense of warmth that he had not experienced since his time in Diamond Bone Village many years ago. After three days, the travel party ran out of food. After some discussion, the Commander decided to kill the couple and keep the child as a hostage. Baron was the only one who opposed this decision and tried to stop them. In response, the soldiers beat him up and tied him up with the child. They then used a powered spear to impale the couple from behind before roasting them alive in front of Baron and the child with a Fire Beast(flamethrower). Barons closed his eyes tightly, not wanting to witness the gruesome scene, but he could still hear the child's cries and smell the burning flesh. He was filled with terror and began trembling uncontrollably. The Commander saw him as weak and ordered the soldiers to force-feed him and the child two pieces of crispy chicken meat. Barron was severely traumatized, became mentally unstable and experienced some changes in his brain. Whenever he closed his eyes, he would see the black stone from before with such clarity that it was frightening, and eventually he became too afraid to close his eyes at all. That night, the soldier on duty noticed Barron's wide-eyed stare and approached him to investigate, but as it turned out, Barron suddenly went berserk and knocked the soldier down with his head. He then used his power spear to break free from his restraints, grabbed a flamethrower, and roasted every sleeping person until they were crispy and smelled like chicken. After the massacre, Barron gave some roasted chicken to a child and reminded him to eat it, then released him. After safely returning to the base with a bunch of roasted chicken, Barron was questioned by the supreme leader. He came up with a few excuses and offered all the cooked chicken as tribute. The supreme leader felt that he had suffered enough and didn't pursue the matter further, revoking his punishment for being forcibly drafted into the army. However, when Barron thought that the incident was over, the nightmare from many years ago returned to haunt him in his dreams. This time, in addition to his first love and the little girl, there were also sounds of children crying and the smell of chicken meat. The situation became increasingly severe, and sometimes he would still see the black stone even when he closed his eyes. In the end, even the doctors were helpless and could only tell him that he hadn't taken his medication today. After several months of experiencing these nightmares, Barron's behavior began to become increasingly strange and he would often talk to himself in a childlike voice. Some people even spread rumors that he was possessed by an evil spirit, which scared away anyone who dared to approach him. His security guards abandoned him, leaving only the servants who had benefited from his generosity to voluntarily care for him. Despite being haunted by his nightmares, Barron never gave up on his charitable work and never complained. Every day, he prayed to the Creator for compensation for his mistake. Years went by, and one day while resting at the mansion, Barron saw his first love appear before him once again. This time, her head wasn't exploding like in his nightmares, and Barron knew it was just an illusion. However, he was overcome with emotions and ran towards her crying uncontrollably, confessing his mistakes and hoping she could kill him as compensation. But she didn't say a word and just left, leaving Barron in tears. After Baron's disappointment, he returned to bed and waited for the nightmare to come again. At that moment, a servant rushed in to inform him of an intrusion, and he realized that the woman from earlier was not a hallucination. He regained his will to live and became excited. Although he still couldn't sleep that night, it was due to excitement rather than fear. Since that day, Baron's mental illness had been cured, and he no longer had nightmares. He didn't waste any time and used his wealth and intelligence network to search for the woman. In just a few days, he discovered the destruction of the Diamond Bones laboratory and its connection to most of the people involved. With this information, he soon learned that the woman from that night was his first love's daughter, the little girl he had caused to be broken and shattered. Her father had saved her by turning her into a cyborg, and Baron saw this as an opportunity for the Creator to compensate him. As Baron was filled with hope, one piece of information made him nervous. The intelligence showed that the Diamond Bone Circle was gathering a group of knights and they were about to slaughter the researchers' village. Baron knew that the girl's life was in danger and he urgently needed a troop. However, he had already lost the recruitment rights of the Flame Legion and could only use his wealth to recruit mercenaries. For this purpose, he went to Jaeger's territory. However, the recruitment process was not smooth due to time constraints and lack of target numbers. His mission was perceived as a suicide mission, even after offering multiple times the usual payment rate, he failed to attract any soldiers to join him. The ticking clock made Baron increasingly anxious. Just when he was considering raising the price again, a sniper known as the Queen appeared in front of him with a hundred soldiers. As per the intelligence report, the woman had led troops to destroy a laboratory and executed prisoners. Looking at her serious demeanor, Baron was terrified. He assumed he had interfered with Jaeger and Diamond Bone's diplomacy. However, the situation was not what he expected. The Queen had received the same intelligence but due to personal reasons, she couldn't lead her troops. Instead, she prepared a force for him, charging only half of the fee, on the condition that all captives must belong to Jaeger. Jaeger was a notorious human trafficking organization, so Baron became extremely nervous upon hearing this. He immediately raised the issue of the girl to the Queen. After learning about the situation, the Queen agreed to let him handle the girl's case independently. Although the situation became very strange, Baron did not have time to think too much. He immediately set off with his troops towards the town. Along the way, Baron knew that the number of knights who were going to slaughter the village could not match theirs, so they just needed to hurry up and arrive to achieve their goal. What surprised Baron was that the mercenaries had all taken Wake-up pills, which indicated that the queen had been prepared for this matter. After two days of rushing, they finally arrived near the town at night and saw the fleeing townspeople. Baron intercepted them, but the townspeople recognized him as the attacker from years ago, causing even more confusion. It wasn't until Baron explained his intentions that they calmed down a bit. At this point, a townsman informed Baron that the girl was fighting against the knights alone. In a hurry, he ordered the mercenaries to run towards the town and fired warning shots into the air. The townspeople who saw this rushed forward to lead the way. Baron watched them rush into the town and desperately tried to catch up while praying that tragedy would not happen again. After Baron entered the town, the situation had already been brought under control by the Jaegers. Although the girl was left with a severely damaged body, she remained conscious and gave him a shocked expression, allowing him to finally breathe a sigh of relief. Baron kept his promise and brought everyone back to the Jaeger's territory. On the way, he explained the reason for their capture to the girl, but he could see the hatred in her eyes. He knew that he couldn't expect forgiveness for his past mistakes right away, but he believed that persistence would eventually pay off. Upon returning to the Jaeger's territory, Baron received criticism for forcibly taking the villagers as prisoners, and he felt uneasy about the Queen's intentions. However, what happened next surprised him. The Queen only requested that the villagers join the Jaegers, and the Diamond Bone knights were only placed under soft detention. Although they were technically prisoners, they were actually being protected. Baron didn't fully understand what the sniper was up to, but after this experience, he realized that the Queen wasn't as terrifying as the rumors made her out to be. He also understood that taking the girl back to the Flame Legion would only cause trouble, so he rented a workshop from the Queen to repair her instead. he process was just as Baron had anticipated - the young girl refused his repairs and even lashed out at him. But Baron knew her injuries were not simple; her whole body needed to be replaced, and this was a dangerous procedure that could not be handled by ordinary bionics technology or funding. He also knew he did not want to live in the nightmare anymore, and this young girl was his only salvation. So he persisted in persuading her, perhaps because he was just too annoying, and eventually she agreed, with the condition that no one would bump her head. Baron was overjoyed and contacted his medical team, who then took care of the basic surgery while Baron prepared a gift for the girl. He had combined the knowledge of Diamond Bone Biomedical Engineering and Star Explorer Technology to develop a set of bionic neural systems. This time, he wanted to apply it to the girl so that she could feel like an ordinary human being. Though it cost him a great deal of effort and expense, seeing the excitement on the girl's face made it all worthwhile. The young girl needed time to adapt to her new neural system, so Baron stepped back from his team's daily hospital operations to personally oversee her rehabilitation. During their long period of interaction, Baron noticed a change in the way the girl looked at him - there was no longer any hatred in her eyes. He even considered taking her in as his adopted daughter to make up for what he had done and care for her as a form of compensation. Feeling grateful for her forgiveness, Baron began urging the girl to repair her damaged face. Although she agreed, she only allowed him to use ordinary mechanical components for the repairs. Upon hearing this, Baron understood the underlying message - every time he saw her half-mechanical face, he was reminded of his first love that he had shot in the head and killed. He felt that the girl had not yet forgiven him and he should continue to live with guilt, so he did not bring up the idea of adoption. While repairing the young girl's head, Baron discovered a hidden memory unit. From its complex settings, he knew it must be something important, but he had no intention of snooping and instead handed it over to the girl directly. Soon after, she approached him to discuss how to fight against Diamond Bone, which made him extremely worried. By this point, Baron had already come to see the girl as his own daughter and did not want her to encounter any more danger. He warned her that going up against the Circle would lead to war and that she did not have the resources to compete with such a massive force. He hoped she would choose to give up, but all he saw was disappointment on her face. Clearly, she had gone through many things that he did not understand. In the end, Baron had no choice but to explain the unreasonable actions of the Jaegers to her, hoping that she could find a solution from there. After that discussion, the young girl left without a word. Baron believed she still resented him and did not dare to look for her again. He only learned of her news through news and intelligence reports. To his surprise, the girl's achievements were remarkable - she liberated several Diamond Bone villages and won over several knight orders. People in other areas also followed her example, causing Diamond Bone to face divisions. The leather and meat industries were severely damaged as a result, and eventually, Star Explorer intervened to force both sides to sign a ceasefire agreement. The whole event lasted more than half a year, and Diamond Bone's power was greatly diminished. Perhaps this is the vulnerability under the fear of Circle's rule. Baron lost track of the girl after the conclusion of the event, only knowing that the rebels were taken over by the knight commander and she disappeared without a trace. Concerned for her safety, Baron was considering going to investigate when the girl appeared at his mansion again. She asked him to repair her half-mechanical face and promised never to leave him again. Overwhelmed with emotion, Baron wept tears of joy upon hearing this. His unwavering efforts finally won the girl's forgiveness, and the nightmares of many years dissipated into nothingness. He promised the Creator that he would cherish this opportunity and vowed to treat the girl like his own daughter. *Baron's life was full of unexpected twists and turns, and his many life experiences led him to tell stories that were equally surprising.
